sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: US Reportedly Working On Draft Regulations For Power Over Nvidia, AMD Chip Exports
feed.summary: The U.S. government is reportedly drafting regulations to gain power over global AI chip exports, which would require companies like Nvidia and AMD to seek approval from the Commerce Department for sales. These rules aim to monitor the AI industry rather than ban exports, with approval dependent on computing power volume and potentially strict security requirements for large deployments. This move is part of the U.S.'s effort to establish dominance in the AI race with China, although the framework is still subject to change.

feed.title: Trump Admin Weighs New Export Rules That Ties Chip Purchase From Likes Of Nvidia, AMD To Investments In US Data Centers: Report
feed.summary: The Trump administration is reportedly considering new export rules for advanced AI chips from companies like Nvidia and AMD. The proposed framework could require foreign governments purchasing large quantities of these chips to invest in U.S. data centers or provide security guarantees. This policy would mark a departure from the Biden-era approach and aims to prevent chip diversion to China while supporting the secure development of AI supercomputers.

feed.title: Marvell Technology, Inc. Reports Fourth Quarter and Fiscal Year 2026 Financial Results
feed.summary: Marvell Technology, Inc. reported record financial results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year 2026, driven by strong AI demand. The company achieved $2.219 billion in Q4 net revenue and $8.195 billion for the full fiscal year, with GAAP diluted income per share of $0.46 and $3.07 respectively. Marvell also provided a positive outlook for the first quarter of fiscal 2027, anticipating continued growth in its data center business.

feed.title: Korea launches AI network alliance at MWC in 6G push
feed.summary: South Korea has formed a public-private AI network alliance (AINA) at MWC 2026 to secure leadership in AI-powered 6G networks and achieve 6G commercialization by 2030. The alliance, chaired by KT Corp. and including 34 companies like SK telecom and Samsung Electronics, aims to increase Korea's share of the global next-generation mobile communications market to 20 percent. This initiative comes amidst a global race to develop AI-driven telecom networks, with other major alliances also being formed by companies from the US, China, and various multinational corporations.

feed.title: SambaNova Introduces New Chip for Agentic AI in Collaboration with Intel
feed.summary: SambaNova has introduced its new SN50 AI chip, designed for agentic AI, which is five times faster than competitive chips and offers a 3X lower total cost of ownership. The company announced a collaboration with Intel to provide high-performance, cost-efficient AI inference solutions, and secured over $350 million in Series E financing. This investment will expand manufacturing and cloud capacity, with SoftBank Corp. being the first customer to deploy the SN50 in its Japanese AI data centers for Asia-Pacific clients.

feed.title: Jabil Inc. Aktie (ISIN US46612W1036): Electronic manufacturing heavyweight in focus for global inves
feed.summary: This article examines Jabil Inc. (ISIN US46612W1036), a key global electronics manufacturing and supply chain partner. feed.title: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Ltd. $TSM Stock Holdings Trimmed by Pitcairn Co.

feed.title: Mini AI PC from Nano Labs runs open-source assistant platform

This compact device is designed for diverse uses, including gaming, professional content creation, and smart offices, emphasizing a localized AI experience. The company also plans to introduce iPollo Claw OS, a Skill Hub, and more OpenClaw-compatible hardware to build an integrated ecosystem.

feed.title: White Falcon Capital Management’s Views on Advanced Micro Devices (AMD)
feed.summary: White Falcon Capital Management's Q4 2025 investor letter highlighted Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) as a key holding, noting its role as a high-performance computing leader. Despite AMD's volatility, White Falcon Capital sees strong long-term prospects, although they suggest other AI stocks might offer greater upside with less risk.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Down 16% Since FQ4 2025 Earnings, Here’s Why

feed.title: Brokers Suggest Investing in Celestica (CLS): Read This Before Placing a Bet

feed.title: Martin Shkreli's New Computing Firm Is Betting It Can Upend Nvidia's Business Model - NVIDIA (NASDAQ:NVDA)
feed.summary: Martin Shkreli, known as "Pharma Bro," has joined Q/C Technologies Inc. as a strategic advisor and is promoting photonic computing as a technology that could disrupt Nvidia's GPU dominance in AI hardware. Shkreli argues that photonic computing, which uses light instead of electrical signals, can perform matrix multiplication (a core GPU function) much faster. While financial markets currently show skepticism about photonic computing as a near-term threat, Shkreli believes it could significantly impact the GPU market and related investment assumptions.

feed.title: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Demands Rise as AI-Ready Consumer Devices Surge in Asia
feed.summary: The demand for semiconductors from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) and other Asian chipmakers is surging due to the increasing integration of AI functionalities into consumer devices like smartphones and laptops. This trend, driven by a shift in consumer behavior towards AI-capable hardware, has led to a significant boost in earnings and orders for these companies. Key regions like Taiwan, Japan, South Korea, Singapore, and Malaysia are adapting to this demand by expanding production, improving supply chains, and investing in specialized AI-specific chip components.

feed.title: Oracle, OpenAI Scrap Texas AI Data Center Expansion Plans; Meta Eyes Site: Report
feed.summary: Oracle and OpenAI have reportedly abandoned their plans to expand an AI data center in Abilene, Texas, due to financing issues and OpenAI's changing demand forecasts. This unexpected development has opened the door for Meta Platforms to consider leasing the site. Nvidia played a role in facilitating discussions between Meta and the data center developer, Crusoe, with Nvidia having previously paid a significant deposit for the expansion.

feed.title: Aehr Receives Follow-On Order for Fully Automated Wafer-Level Burn-In Systems Powering AI Optical I/O and Data Center Interconnects

feed.title: Entero's Grid AI Prepares for Major U.S. Expansion with New Leadership Appointments to Accelerate AI Infrastructure Strategy
feed.summary: Entero Therapeutics' subsidiary, Grid AI, is preparing for a significant expansion into the U.S. market for AI-ready digital infrastructure by establishing a new U.S.-based leadership team. The company expects to announce a U.S. Chief Executive Officer and Executive Chairman soon, bringing deep public-markets experience to accelerate Grid AI's strategy of integrating AI orchestration and energy optimization. This move comes as the global data-center market is projected to reach $1 trillion by 2030, requiring advanced control systems and AI-driven solutions which are core to Grid AI's platform.

feed.title: Entero's Grid AI Prepares for Major U.S. Expansion with New Leadership Appointments to Accelerate AI Infrastructure Strategy
feed.summary: Entero's Grid AI, a subsidiary of Entero Therapeutics, is finalizing the establishment of a U.S.-based leadership team to accelerate its expansion into the rapidly growing U.S. market for AI-ready digital infrastructure. This move comes as the data center market is projected to reach $1 trillion globally by 2030, requiring thousands of new data centers. The new leadership will focus on integrating AI orchestration, energy optimization, and digital infrastructure controls for major AI deployments.

feed.title: STMicroelectronics enters high-volume production of its industry-leading silicon photonics platform to support AI infrastructure demand
feed.summary: STMicroelectronics has begun high-volume production of its PIC100 silicon photonics platform to meet the growing demands of AI infrastructure and data centers. This platform provides 800G and 1.6T transceivers, offering increased bandwidth, lower latency, and better energy efficiency for AI workloads. The company plans to significantly expand production capacity by 2027 and will introduce the PIC100 TSV platform for future optical-electronic integration.

feed.title: EDENTREE ASSET MANAGEMENT Ltd Increases Position in NVIDIA Corporation $NVDA
feed.summary: EDENTREE ASSET MANAGEMENT Ltd significantly increased its stake in NVIDIA Corporation by 1,355.9% to 4,950 shares, now valued at $924,000, as institutional ownership of the company stands at 65.27%. NVIDIA reported strong quarterly earnings with an EPS of $1.62 and revenue up 73.2% year-over-year, leading analysts to maintain a "Buy" rating and raise price targets. Key risks include potential U.S. export restrictions on AI chips and NVIDIA's scaling back of H200 production for China, which could impact international sales.

feed.title: Adeia Enters into Multi-Year IP License Agreement with AMD
feed.summary: Adeia Inc. announced a multi-year intellectual property license agreement with Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), providing AMD access to Adeia's semiconductor IP portfolio. This agreement also resolves all existing litigation between the two companies. Adeia's CEO, Paul E. Davis, expressed satisfaction with the resolution, highlighting the potential for future collaborations on advanced semiconductor technologies.

feed.title: Diversify Advisory Services LLC Sells 69,424 Shares of Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. $AMD

feed.title: Qt Group joins forces with Qualcomm to speed up development of Industrial AI devices for future factories
feed.summary: Qt Group has partnered with Qualcomm to accelerate the development of Industrial AI devices for future factories. This collaboration involves pre-optimizing the Qt framework for Qualcomm Technologies' Dragonwing IQ series processors, simplifying the creation of Edge AI devices by abstracting complex hardware and AI programming. The initiative aims to enable businesses to deploy smart factory applications faster and facilitate innovation in industrial automation.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC is offering Callable Contingent Interest Notes linked to the least performing of the Dow Jones Industrial Average®, the Russell 2000® Index, and the VanEck® Semiconductor ETF, due February 14, 2028. These structured investments, fully and unconditionally guaranteed by JPMorgan Chase & Co., are designed for investors seeking contingent interest payments when the underlying assets meet certain conditions, but carry the risk of principal loss and no guaranteed interest. The notes may be redeemed early at JPMorgan Financial's option and are subject to various risks, including credit risk and volatility of the underlying indices and ETF.

feed.title: Dynamic Technology Lab Boosts Stake in Silicon Laboratories
feed.summary: Dynamic Technology Lab Private Ltd has significantly increased its stake in Silicon Laboratories (NASDAQ:SLAB) by 255.1% in the third quarter, now owning 7,030 shares worth $922,000. This substantial investment suggests the institutional investor has strong confidence in the semiconductor company's future performance and growth potential, particularly in high-growth markets like the Internet of Things and industrial automation.

feed.title: TELO Stock News Today | Earnings, Events & Price Alerts
feed.summary: This article provides recent news and events related to Telomir Pharmaceuticals (TELO) stock, focusing on its research into triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC). Key announcements include new in vitro data showing Telomir-1 induces tumor cell mortality in TNBC, cellular study results demonstrating Telomir-Zn's ability to redistribute intracellular zinc and iron, and efficacy study results in zebrafish models suggesting Telomir-1 can significantly reduce tumor growth and cancer cell spread in aggressive TNBC.

feed.title: Can Intel's AI Push With Infosys Deal Drive Long-Term Growth?
feed.summary: Intel Corporation is expanding its enterprise AI strategy through a deeper partnership with Infosys Limited to help businesses deploy AI solutions at scale. This collaboration combines Intel’s computing infrastructure with Infosys’ AI software, optimizing AI workloads on Intel's hardware and enabling secure, reliable AI systems. Despite facing strong competition from NVIDIA and AMD in the AI space, Intel aims to capitalize on the rapidly growing AI market by providing cutting-edge hardware and software solutions.

feed.title: LGT Group Foundation Has $8.98 Million Holdings in Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. $AMD

feed.title: Free LA COLORfest pop-up offers $100 in Sally Beauty products
feed.summary: Sally Beauty is launching "COLORfest," a month-long campaign emphasizing self-expression through hair and nail color, starting with a free pop-up event on March 14, 2026, at The Grove in Los Angeles. This event will offer personalized color analysis, expert consultations, product demonstrations, and gift bundles valued at $100. COLORfest, planned to occur twice annually, aims to blend accessibility with education, empowering beauty enthusiasts to experiment with color.

feed.title: Intel Corp Stock (INTC) Opened Down by 3.27% on Mar 9: What Signal Does It Send?
feed.summary: Intel Corp (INTC) stock opened down by 3.27% on March 9, driven by concerns over manufacturing capacity, low 18A process yields, and the company's struggle to meet AI-driven demand. Regulatory scrutiny linked to China-related chip tools and cautious analyst sentiment further contributed to the decline. Intel's CFO and Bank of America analysts present conflicting views on 18A yields, adding to investor uncertainty.

feed.title: Adeia (NASDAQ: ADEA) inks multi-year AMD IP license, ends litigation
feed.summary: Adeia and AMD have signed a multi-year intellectual property license agreement, granting AMD access to Adeia's semiconductor IP portfolio, including technologies for hybrid bonding, packaging, and processing. This agreement also resolves all ongoing litigation between the two companies, allowing both to move forward and potentially explore future collaborations. Adeia's CEO highlighted the strategic value of this resolution and its potential to strengthen Adeia's role as a key IP provider to leading chipmakers.

(NASDAQ:ADEA) to $40 from $30, maintaining a Buy rating, following a new multi-year intellectual property licensing agreement with Advanced Micro Devices (AMD). feed.title: Dell Technologies Climbs 16% YTD: Should You Buy the Stock?
feed.summary: Dell Technologies (DELL) stock has gained significantly year-to-date, outperforming its industry peers, driven by expanding AI infrastructure demand. The company boasts a strong financial position, with substantial operating cash flow and a record backlog for AI-optimized servers, and plans to further invest in AI capabilities. Despite its strong performance and growth prospects, DELL stock is currently trading at a lower valuation compared to the industry average, making it an attractive "Buy" recommendation from Zacks Investment Research.

feed.title: Archer accuses Joby of hiding China supply chain in explosive eVTOL counterclaim
feed.summary: Archer Aviation has filed a sweeping counterclaim against rival eVTOL developer Joby Aviation, alleging that Joby concealed Chinese supply chains and misled US regulators. Archer claims Joby misclassified imported aircraft components from China as consumer goods, evading tariffs and improperly securing government contracts. This legal escalation is part of an ongoing dispute in the competitive eVTOL sector, where companies like Archer, Joby, and Vertical Aerospace are battling for market dominance, with legal strategies becoming integral to their competitive playbooks.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on Take-Two Interactive: "I Think You've Gotta Get In Before GTA"
feed.summary: Jim Cramer recommends buying Take-Two Interactive (TTWO) stock before the launch of Grand Theft Auto VI, suggesting investors "get in before GTA" due to a recent dip. He views the stock as a "great level" for investment, despite previous concerns he noted about AI potentially disrupting the gaming industry and affecting companies like Take-Two. Cramer believes the company, as the last independent video game publisher in America, is undervalued.

feed.title: ONEOK, Inc. (OKE) Price Target Raised by $5 Following Q4 Report
feed.summary: Jefferies raised its price target on ONEOK, Inc. (OKE) from $80 to $85 after the company's Q4 report, while maintaining a 'Hold' rating. The analyst noted that ONEOK's FY 2026 adjusted EBITDA outlook of $8.1 billion is flat compared to the previous year, despite new synergies. This weak guidance led to a more than 5% drop in ONEOK's stock, though Jefferies believes it "appropriately de-risked" the stock.

feed.title: Adeia settles semiconductor patent lawsuits against AMD
feed.summary: Technology licensing company Adeia has settled two patent lawsuits against Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) regarding semiconductor technology. AMD will take a multi-year license to Adeia's semiconductor IP portfolio for an undisclosed sum. The lawsuits, filed in November, alleged that several AMD processor models, including those with "3D V-Cache" technology used in AI chips, infringed on Adeia's patents related to improved semiconductor manufacturing.

feed.title: AMD (AMD) Stock Is Up, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) jumped 2.7% after the company announced an expansion of its Ryzen AI Embedded P100 Series processors and resolved a patent dispute with Adeia Inc. through a multi-year intellectual property license agreement. The new processors are designed for edge AI applications, while the licensing deal removes a significant source of uncertainty for the company. AMD's stock closed up 5.4% at $202.69, reflecting market positivity towards these strategic developments and their potential impact on AMD's AI computing market capture.

feed.title: PlusAI launches its upgraded autonomous driver for freight operations
feed.summary: PlusAI has released SuperDrive 6.0, an upgraded autonomous driving software for commercial freight operations, ahead of its planned public listing. The new version enables capabilities such as night driving and construction zone handling, aiming for 24/7 autonomous operations. SuperDrive 6.0 also boasts improved cost-efficiency in development and faster deployment of features, utilizing advanced AI and a distributed compute architecture.

feed.title: AMD Extends Ryzen AI Embedded P100 Series for Edge AI Applications
feed.summary: AMD has expanded its Ryzen AI Embedded P100 series with new Zen 5-based processors, featuring up to 12 cores and enhanced GPU/NPU performance for edge AI applications. These processors offer improved multithreaded performance, significant TOPS for AI inference, and robust reliability features designed for industrial, robotics, and medical sectors. Sampling is currently underway, with production slated to begin in July 2026.

feed.title: AMD Stock Jumps 5.33% with $7.63B in Trading Volume, Ranking 11th in Market Activity Following Settlement of Adeia Patent Conflict
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock surged 5.33% with $7.63 billion in trading volume following the resolution of a patent conflict with Adeia Inc. The multi-year licensing agreement grants AMD access to Adeia's intellectual property, removing legal risks and enhancing AMD's ability to develop AI chips and advanced manufacturing processes. This, combined with broader semiconductor market recovery and institutional investor confidence, has bolstered AMD's long-term growth prospects.

feed.title: Q2 holdings CPO Rutledge sells shares worth $1.3m
feed.summary: Kimberly Anne Rutledge, Chief People Officer of Q2 Holdings, Inc. (QTWO), sold 25,288 shares of company stock in two transactions on March 5 and March 9, 2026, totaling approximately $1.3 million. These sales occurred near the stock's 52-week low. Despite the share sales and recent EPS miss, analysts have revised earnings upwards, and the stock is trading close to its Fair Value.

feed.title: NVIDIA or Advanced Micro Devices: Which Stock Has More Upside?
feed.summary: This article analyzes whether NVIDIA (NVDA) or Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) offers more upside for investors. Based on revenue growth, profitability, and valuation, the analysis suggests that NVDA is a more favorable investment compared to AMD. The article provides detailed financial comparisons and historical market performance data to support its conclusion, while also recommending a portfolio approach to investing.

feed.title: AMD Stock Rises 5.32% on Edge AI Expansion and Legal Settlement
feed.summary: AMD stock climbed 5.32% after announcing a strategic expansion into Edge AI and resolving a long-standing legal dispute. This expansion includes new processor series for industrial automation and robotics and a licensing pact with Adeia Inc. The positive developments, alongside strong demand for CPUs, reflect increased investor confidence, though the stock saw a slight dip after hours.

feed.title: Green Hills Software Creates Complete Production-Focused Software Environment for Texas Instruments TDA5 Virtual Development Kit
feed.summary: Green Hills Software has launched a comprehensive, production-focused software development environment for Texas Instruments' new TDA5 SoC family, designed for virtual development platforms. This SDK, developed in collaboration with TI and Synopsys, includes safety-certified RTOSes, a hypervisor, and multicore development tools to accelerate the development of software-defined vehicles. It is unique in its support for all Arm processor core complexes on the TDA5 SoC, allowing vehicle manufacturers to begin production-focused software development before silicon availability, reducing costs and development time.

feed.title: Banque Cantonale Vaudoise Buys 3,571 Shares of Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. $AMD
feed.summary: Banque Cantonale Vaudoise increased its stake in Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) by 5.5% in Q3, purchasing 3,571 shares to hold a total of 68,449 shares valued at approximately $11.07 million. AMD reported strong Q3 earnings with an EPS beat and significant revenue growth, while also settling a multi-year IP licensing dispute with Adeia. Despite positive developments like product expansion and institutional buying, the company faces potential regulatory and geopolitical supply chain risks.

feed.title: AMD Investment Positions Nutanix To Pursue Larger Enterprise AI Infrastructure Deals
feed.summary: AMD has invested in Nutanix to form a multi-year partnership aimed at building an open, full-stack AI infrastructure platform for enterprises. This collaboration combines Nutanix software with AMD's AI-focused hardware, providing Nutanix with capital and technical resources to pursue larger AI infrastructure deals. The partnership is expected to strengthen Nutanix's position in the enterprise AI market, although risks like high debt and potential vendor concentration exist.

feed.title: New QNX Hypervisor targets safer cars, robots and medical gear
feed.summary: BlackBerry QNX has launched its safety-certified Hypervisor 8.0 for Safety, a virtualization platform designed for mixed-criticality Physical AI systems in automotive, medical, and robotics sectors. The hypervisor meets stringent functional safety requirements including ISO 26262 ASIL D and IEC 61508 SIL 4, offering deterministic timing and fault-tolerant isolation. Early customer commitments include a leading Chinese automaker and a European healthcare company, highlighting the platform's value in modernizing device architectures and accelerating regulatory-compliant development.

feed.title: STMicroelectronics propels new era of ultra-wideband technology for automotive and smart device applications
feed.summary: STMicroelectronics has launched the ST64UWB family, a new line of ultra-wideband (UWB) chips that support the next-generation wireless standard, IEEE 802.15.4ab. These chips offer extended range, improved processing power, and robustness for various automotive, consumer, and industrial applications, including secure digital access, presence detection, and enhanced radar modes like child presence detection in vehicles. The ST64UWB family leverages ST's 18 nm FD-SOI technology to deliver industry-leading RF performance and is currently sampling to major Tier 1s and original equipment manufacturers.

feed.title: Can Himax Technologies’ (HIMX) Ultralow Power AI Shift Its Competitive Position In Edge Computing?
feed.summary: Himax Technologies showcased its WiseEye ultralow power endpoint AI, automotive display ICs, and optical solutions at Embedded World 2026, highlighting the WiseGuard and PalmVein modules for low-power biometric security. While these developments reinforce the company's long-term investment narrative, immediate challenges remain due to cyclical weakness in certain sectors and uncertainties from potential new US tariffs on semiconductor components, which could impact supply chain resilience and customer demand. Analysts project Himax to achieve $1.1 billion in revenue and $139.3 million in earnings by 2028, suggesting a potential 11% upside from its current valuation.

feed.title: Nvidia (NVDA) or Micron (MU)? Morgan Stanley Reinstates Its Top AI Chip Pick
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley analyst Joseph Moore has reinstated Nvidia (NVDA) as the firm's top semiconductor pick for AI chips, replacing Micron Technology (MU). Moore cited Nvidia's strong business growth and relatively flat stock performance, contrasting it with Micron's significant recent surge. He believes Nvidia's stock remains attractive due to its valuation and continued strong demand for AI GPUs, while investor concerns about AI spending and custom chips are overblown.

feed.title: Comfort Systems USA, Inc. $FIX Stock Position Lifted by Jefferies Financial Group Inc.

Despite a dividend increase to $0.70 quarterly, insiders have sold 37,288 shares worth about $53.7 million over the past 90 days, bringing insider ownership to 1.59%.

feed.title: Why Is Taiwan Semiconductor Stock Gaining Tuesday? - Taiwan Semiconductor (NYSE:TSM)
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. Ltd. (TSM) reported significant sales growth in the first two months of the year, driven by strong demand for AI infrastructure, contributing to its stock's gain on Tuesday. This rise comes despite broader semiconductor market volatility caused by new U.S. export policy discussions and Middle East conflicts hindering supply chains. The company's revenue reached $22.6 billion for January and February, with a 22% increase in February sales.

feed.title: Sanmina Benefits From AI Focus: Should You Join the Bandwagon?
feed.summary: Sanmina (SANM) is strategically positioning itself as a key enabler in the AI ecosystem by expanding its capabilities in AI data center infrastructure, notably through the acquisition of ZT Systems. The company is leveraging advanced engineering and connected manufacturing (42Q) to support its growth initiatives and has seen significant positive revisions in its earnings estimates. Despite facing stiff competition and geopolitical challenges, Sanmina is expected to continue its growth trajectory due to its strong AI focus and technological leadership.

feed.title: What's Going On With AMD Stock Tuesday? - Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD)
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is expanding its partnerships and product offerings, with its stock rebounding despite geopolitical concerns and potential new export rules for AI chips. BlackBerry's QNX division is broadening its support for AMD processors, while Adeia Inc. has entered a multi-year licensing agreement with AMD, resolving past litigation. AMD shares rallied on Monday after a recent sector-wide selloff.

feed.title: Xanadu and AMD Accelerate Quantum Computing for Aerospace and Engineering
feed.summary: Xanadu Quantum Technologies Inc. and AMD have announced a significant advancement in quantum computing for aerospace and engineering. Their collaboration demonstrated how hybrid quantum-classical environments, leveraging Xanadu's PennyLane quantum software and AMD's HPC solutions, can prepare and run advanced aerospace simulations. This partnership aims to transition quantum computing from research to industrial use, showcasing a 25-fold reduction in simulation time for a core quantum algorithm by utilizing AMD GPUs.

feed.title: Legal & General Group Plc Increases Stake in Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. $AMD
feed.summary: Legal & General Group Plc increased its stake in Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) by 1.7% in Q3, now owning 11,579,113 shares valued at approximately $1.87 billion. Other institutional investors like Norges Bank and Vanguard Group also made significant moves, with institutions owning 71.34% of AMD stock. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus for AMD with an average price target of $290.53, citing company catalysts such as an IP-license settlement with Adeia and expansion of its Ryzen AI desktop lineup.

feed.title: Capital World Investors Purchases 21,230,715 Shares of Intel Corporation $INTC
feed.summary: Capital World Investors increased its stake in Intel Corporation by 32.5%, acquiring over 21 million additional shares, bringing its total holdings to 86.5 million shares valued at approximately $2.90 billion. While Intel beat earnings and revenue estimates, its revenue declined year-over-year, and analysts maintain a "Reduce" rating with a target price of $45.74. Institutional investors collectively own 64.53% of Intel, and the company is navigating both positive sentiment from AI partnerships and caution regarding competitive dynamics and execution risks.

feed.title: Rackspace–Uniphore AI deal cuts pilot-to-production time to weeks
feed.summary: Rackspace Technology (NASDAQ: RXT) and Uniphore have announced a strategic partnership to deliver the industry's first Infrastructure-to-Agents architecture as an outcomes-based service, targeting $100 million in enterprise AI deployments. This collaboration aims to accelerate AI adoption in regulated industries by combining Uniphore’s Business AI Cloud with Rackspace’s private cloud, offering a secure and governed full-stack AI solution. The service shortens the path from AI pilot to production to weeks, includes Data Preparation-as-a-Service, fine-tuned Small Language Models (SLMs)-as-a-Service, and features forward-deployed engineers to ensure measurable business outcomes.

feed.title: Van ECK Associates Corp Reduces Holdings in Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. $AMD
feed.summary: Van ECK Associates Corp reduced its stake in Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) by 7.3% in Q3, selling over 900,000 shares but still holding 0.71% of the company, valued at approximately $1.86 billion. Despite this reduction, other major institutional investors like Vanguard Group and State Street Corp increased their holdings. AMD recently reported stronger-than-expected earnings, with EPS of $1.53 against a consensus of $1.32 and revenue of $10.27 billion, contributing to its "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts.

feed.title: Intel Corporation (INTC) and Infosys Expand Strategic AI Infrastructure Partnership
feed.summary: Intel Corporation (INTC) and Infosys are expanding their strategic collaboration to help enterprises transition AI initiatives from pilot programs to full-scale production. This partnership combines Intel’s compute platforms with Infosys’ Topaz Fabric to deliver scalable AI infrastructure, focusing on open standards and secure, cost-efficient AI deployments. Additionally, Intel is collaborating with Ericsson to accelerate ecosystem readiness for AI-native 6G networks, highlighting Intel's growing role in AI and telecommunications infrastructure.

feed.title: Solar gear maker Nextpower targets 58.8% emissions cut by 2035
feed.summary: Nextpower (NASDAQ: NXT) has received validation from the Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi) for its near-term carbon emissions reduction targets. The company commits to cutting absolute Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 58.8% by fiscal year 2035, and reducing Scope 3 emissions intensity by 63.8% over the same period, demonstrating its commitment to measurable climate leadership. These goals are supported by Nextpower's efforts in optimizing supply chains, increasing domestic manufacturing, and innovating with lower-carbon products like the NX Horizon tracker.

feed.title: Applied Materials partners with Micron to develop next-gen AI memory products in US
feed.summary: Applied Materials and Micron Technology have announced a partnership to develop next-generation memory products, including DRAM, high-bandwidth memory, and NAND solutions. The collaboration aims to enhance the performance of artificial intelligence systems and strengthen the semiconductor innovation pipeline in the US.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Stock Rises 5% on Xanadu Quantum Partnership
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock increased by 5.33% following its designation as a lead investor in Xanadu's $275 million quantum computing funding round. The partnership achieved a breakthrough by using hybrid quantum-classical computing for aerospace simulations, reducing processing time by 25 times. This development comes as Xanadu prepares for its public debut via a SPAC merger with Crane Harbor Acquisition Corp.

feed.title: Target Hospitality: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Target Hospitality (TH) reported a Q4 loss of $14.9 million, or 15 cents per share, missing analyst expectations of a 10 cent loss. However, the company's revenue of $89.8 million exceeded Street forecasts of $85.2 million. For the full year, Target Hospitality reported a $37.1 million loss on $320.6 million in revenue, and expects future annual revenue between $320 million and $330 million.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Stock Surges 5% Following Xanadu Quantum Computing Investment
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock surged over 5% following its lead strategic investment in Xanadu Quantum Computing and a collaboration success. The partnership demonstrated a 25x faster aerospace simulation using Xanadu's PennyLane platform and AMD's high-performance systems. Xanadu is set to go public via a SPAC merger with Crane Harbor Acquisition Corp, listing as "XNDU" on Nasdaq and the Toronto Stock Exchange.

feed.title: Sanmina Benefits From AI Focus: Should You Join the Bandwagon?
feed.summary: Sanmina Corporation is strategically focusing on the artificial intelligence (AI) ecosystem, leveraging acquisitions like ZT Systems to enhance its AI and cloud manufacturing capabilities. Despite strong stock performance and positive earnings estimate revisions, the company faces challenges from stiff competition, international exposure, and high R&D costs. Nonetheless, its technology leadership and AI-focused approach position it for long-term growth, earning it a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy).

feed.title: AMD Surges 109% in a Year: Is There More Room for the Stock to Grow?
feed.summary: AMD has seen its shares surge 109% over the past year, driven by strong demand for its data center EPYC processors and Instinct GPUs. However, analysts believe future stock appreciation may be limited due to intensifying competition from NVIDIA, Broadcom, and Intel, along with a modest guidance for Q1 2026. Despite near-term challenges and a "Hold" rating from Zacks, AMD is expanding its AI data center footprint and AI PC offerings, which are expected to drive long-term top-line growth.

feed.title: Michael Burry Calls Nvidia 'Mafia-Like,' Says AI Chip Market Could Face Antitrust Scrutiny
feed.summary: Michael Burry, known for "The Big Short," has accused Nvidia of "mafia-like" behavior in the AI chip market, alleging it blocked AMD from a major data-center deal related to OpenAI and Oracle. Burry claims Nvidia paid to stop AMD from securing the contract for Blackwell chips and suggests the U.S. Justice Department has been investigating Nvidia for two years. His comments highlight growing tensions and potential antitrust scrutiny in the rapidly expanding AI infrastructure sector.

feed.title: AMD Surges 109% in a Year: Is There More Room for the Stock to Grow?
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) shares have surged 109% over the past year, driven by strong demand for data center EPYC processors and Instinct GPUs. Despite this growth and expectations for future AI data center expansion, the article suggests limited room for further appreciation due to intense competition from NVIDIA, Broadcom, and Intel, as well as a modest near-term guidance and high valuation. AMD is currently rated as a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold).

feed.title: Intel Stock Eyes $47 Breakout During AI-Driven Chip Shortage - Intel (NASDAQ:INTC)
feed.summary: Intel (NASDAQ: INTC) shares are rallying, eyeing a potential breakout above $47, driven by strong AI-fueled demand for server processors that has pushed its factories to full capacity. The semiconductor industry, including Intel, is facing a global chip shortage exacerbated by the AI infrastructure boom and geopolitical tensions impacting supply chains. Additionally, Intel is undergoing a leadership transition with a new independent chair appointed to its board.

feed.title: Crane Harbor Merger Target Xanadu Expands Quantum Work With AMD
feed.summary: Crane Harbor Acquisition Corp's merger target, Xanadu Quantum Technologies, is expanding its quantum computing work with Advanced Micro Devices (AMD). Xanadu utilized AMD's DevCloud for hybrid quantum simulations in computational fluid dynamics, demonstrating significant performance gains and preparing for industrial applications. The merger with Crane Harbor is backed by approximately $500 million in expected proceeds and is anticipated to close in Q1 2026 after a shareholder vote on March 19.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Stock Rises 5% on Xanadu Quantum Partnership
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock rose 5.33% after the chipmaker was named a lead strategic investor in Xanadu's $275 million funding round. This partnership highlights their collaborative use of hybrid quantum-classical computing which achieved a 25x reduction in aerospace simulation time. Xanadu is preparing to go public via a SPAC merger with Crane Harbor Acquisition Corp, and will trade under the ticker "XNDU".

feed.title: 10 Information Technology Stocks Whale Activity In Today's Session
feed.summary: This article identifies significant options activity, referred to as "whale activity," across ten information technology stocks, including NVIDIA, Microsoft, and AMD. It details specific bullish, bearish, or neutral option trades, including contract types, expiration dates, strike prices, and total trade values, to help traders recognize potential market opportunities. The piece also provides a brief explanation of options terminology for clarity.

feed.title: IBM (IBM): Buy, Sell, or Hold Post Q4 Earnings?
feed.summary: This article analyzes IBM's post-Q4 earnings performance, classifying it as a "Hold" rather than a "Buy" or "Sell." The analysis points to disappointing long-term revenue growth (4.1% CAGR over five years) and modest EPS growth (5.9% annually), leading to a cautious outlook despite the company trading at 20.8x forward P/E. The authors suggest looking for alternative investment opportunities in more exciting sectors.

feed.title: SuRo Capital (SSSS) Q4 2025 Earnings Transcript
feed.summary: SuRo Capital (SSSS) reported strong Q4 2025 results, with Net Asset Value (NAV) increasing to $8.09 per share and a total shareholder return of approximately 70%. The company anticipates a significant NAV uplift of $5.00 to $6.50 per share from currently finalizing portfolio financings, particularly in AI infrastructure, though these are not yet reflected in the reported year-end NAV. SuRo Capital emphasized its strategy of investing in high-growth private technology companies, especially within the accelerating AI sector, and plans to continue returning capital to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases.

feed.title: Intel Corporation launches Core Series 2 edge AI chips as turnaround pressure builds
feed.summary: Intel has launched its Core Series 2 processors for industrial edge AI applications and unveiled its Health & Life Sciences Edge AI suite to diversify revenue streams beyond data centers. This move comes as Intel faces pressure to improve growth and meet market expectations, while also competing with AMD and Nvidia in the embedded processor and edge AI hardware markets. The company is navigating challenges including supply constraints, rising memory prices, and political scrutiny over its supply chain.

feed.title: Nvidia makes ‘significant’ investment in Mira Murati’s Thinking Machines
feed.summary: Nvidia Corp. has made a "significant investment" in AI developer Thinking Machines Lab Inc. and entered into a partnership where Thinking Machines will purchase billions of dollars' worth of Nvidia hardware. Thinking Machines, founded by ex-OpenAI CTO Mira Murati, offers Tinker, a cloud service for fine-tuning open-source large language models. The company plans to use Nvidia's latest Rubin GPUs and Vera CPUs, and will also collaborate with Nvidia on developing training and serving systems.

feed.title: AI chipmaker Cerebras namedropped by Oracle, alongside Nvidia and AMD
feed.summary: Cerebras, an AI chipmaker, received a significant endorsement from Oracle, as Oracle's CEO Clay Magouyrk mentioned Cerebras chips are part of their infrastructure alongside Nvidia and AMD. This announcement is a major boost for Cerebras, especially as it plans for an eventual IPO after withdrawing its initial filing due to significant reliance on a single customer. Bolstering its client roster with Oracle and a prior $10 billion commitment from OpenAI helps diversify its revenue and strengthens its market position against larger rivals like Nvidia.

feed.title: New DSP and MCU chip debuts for real-time control, Microchip Technology asserts
feed.summary: Microchip Technology has unveiled a new integrated control chip that combines a Digital Signal Processor (DSP) with a microcontroller unit (MCU). This innovative single-chip architecture is designed to provide high-performance and efficient real-time control solutions for embedded applications. While the announcement highlights enhanced flexibility and improved performance in compact designs, specific details regarding the chip's specifications or its commercial availability were not disclosed.

feed.title: Kronos Worldwide Stock Pre-Market (-5.9%): Reports Much Wider-Than-Expected Q4 Loss
feed.summary: Kronos Worldwide (KRO) reported a much wider-than-expected Q4 loss of -$0.72 per share, significantly missing the consensus estimate and driving a 5.9% pre-market stock decline. The loss was primarily due to an 8% year-over-year drop in selling prices for TiO2 and unabsorbed costs from production cuts, leading to negative gross margins. This performance challenges the bull case for stabilizing TiO2 prices and highlights the company's operational leverage in a downturn.

feed.title: Analysts Offer Insights on Technology Companies: Semtech (SMTC) and STMicroelectronics (STM)
feed.summary: Two technology companies, Semtech (SMTC) and STMicroelectronics (STM), recently received bullish ratings from analysts. Oppenheimer reiterated a Buy rating for Semtech with a $100 price target, highlighting a strong buy consensus and potential 19.5% upside. Robert W. Baird maintained a Buy rating for STMicroelectronics with a $45 price target, indicating a moderate buy consensus and 13.3% upside.

feed.title: Intel Plunges 5.5% Amid Manufacturing Pivot: Breaking Down the $80 Valuation Target
feed.summary: Intel's stock recently dropped 5.5% as the company undergoes a significant manufacturing pivot to reclaim its market position from TSMC. Despite short-term risks and capital expenditures, the company's "18A" process and advanced packaging capabilities are seen as key to its future, with a TIKR Advanced Model target price of $82 based on anticipated margin recovery and increasing CPU demand from agentic AI. The article suggests that while not for the faint of heart, the math supports a historic rebound for Intel as it streamlines operations and capitalizes on new demand drivers.

feed.title: Korean Retail Investors Flock to Leveraged ETFs in U.S. Stock Market
feed.summary: Korean retail investors are increasingly investing in leveraged ETFs tracking U.S.-listed Korean stocks, drawing concern from foreign media who describe it as an "addiction." This trend, particularly notable with the 'Direxion Daily South Korea Bull 3X' (KORU) ETF and semiconductor-focused leveraged ETFs, is driven by market volatility and the semiconductor craze, however, experts warn of amplified stock market volatility and significant risks associated with the daily resetting nature of these products. Financial authorities are monitoring the situation and have implemented measures to inform investors about the heightened risks.

feed.title: Texas Instruments reportedly set to raise prices again
feed.summary: Reports indicate that Texas Instruments is planning another round of price increases, some as high as 85% starting April 1st, for analogue and power management components. Infineon is also expected to raise prices due to explosive demand from AI servers, electric vehicles, and industrial equipment, coupled with ongoing supply chain pressures and rising manufacturing costs. These increases are likely to impact manufacturers, particularly those in PCB assembly, leading to higher production costs and increased market volatility.

feed.title: Applied Materials and Micron Partner To Advance U.S. Innovation in Next-Generation AI Memory Solutions
feed.summary: Applied Materials and Micron Technology are collaborating to develop next-generation AI memory solutions, including advanced DRAM, high-bandwidth memory (HBM), and NAND. This partnership leverages Applied's EPIC Center in Silicon Valley and Micron's innovation center in Boise, Idaho, aiming to accelerate the development of energy-efficient and high-performance memory for AI applications. The initiative seeks to strengthen U.S. leadership in semiconductor innovation through joint R&D and advanced packaging solutions.

feed.title: Analysts Offer Insights on Technology Companies: Allient (ALNT), Oracle (ORCL) and Micron (MU)
feed.summary: This article provides an overview of recent analyst ratings for three technology companies: Allient (ALNT), Oracle (ORCL), and Micron (MU). Analysts maintained ratings for these companies, with Allient receiving a Hold, Oracle a Buy, and Micron a Buy, along with their respective price targets and upside potentials. The insights are based on reports from Northland Securities, Barclays, and UBS, highlighting analyst consensus and individual performance.

feed.title: Capital Research Global Investors Has $321.88 Million Stake in Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. $AMD
feed.summary: Capital Research Global Investors increased its stake in Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) by 0.4% in the third quarter, bringing their total holdings to $321.88 million. Other institutional investors also adjusted their positions, and insider selling activity was reported. The article also provides recent news affecting the company, including potential large deals, product expansions, and analyst ratings, with AMD receiving an average rating of "Moderate Buy."

feed.title: Capital World Investors Lowers Holdings in Comfort Systems USA, Inc. $FIX
feed.summary: Capital World Investors decreased its stake in Comfort Systems USA, Inc. by 19.2% in the third quarter, while several other institutional investors either initiated or increased their positions. Analysts maintain a "Buy" consensus rating on the stock with an average price target of $1,573.67, and the company recently increased its quarterly dividend. Insider transactions show significant sales by SVP Terrence Reed and Director Darcy Anderson in early March.

feed.title: Analysts Are Bullish on Top Technology Stocks: Broadcom (AVGO), Rubrik, Inc. Class A (RBRK)
feed.summary: This article highlights bullish sentiments from financial analysts regarding several technology stocks, specifically Broadcom (AVGO), Rubrik, Inc. Class A (RBRK), and KLA (KLAC). Analysts from DBS and TipRanks – PerPlexity maintain Buy ratings for Broadcom, while Cantor Fitzgerald analysts reiterate Buy ratings for Rubrik and KLA, noting significant upside potential for all three. The reports detail specific price targets and analyst success rates, emphasizing a positive outlook for these companies in the technology sector.

feed.title: Rackspace and Uniphore Announce Strategic Partnership to Define a New Category of Infrastructure-to-Agents Architecture Accelerating Enterprise AI Adoption
feed.summary: Rackspace Technology and Uniphore have announced a strategic partnership to introduce the industry's first Infrastructure-to-Agents architecture as an outcomes-based service. This collaboration aims to accelerate enterprise AI adoption by facilitating the transition from AI experimentation to large-scale production, targeting $100 million in deployments. The partnership emphasizes governance, security, and control, particularly for regulated industries, with Uniphore leveraging support from NVIDIA and AMD.

feed.title: South Korea funds Xanadu-ETRI push to design fault-tolerant quantum algorithms
feed.summary: Xanadu and South Korea's ETRI have launched a two-year collaborative research project, backed by a significant South Korean government grant, to advance software for fault-tolerant quantum computing (FTQC). The partnership aims to enhance PennyLane resource estimation and the Catalyst compiler to optimize qubit and gate usage for distributed FTQC, helping researchers identify algorithmic bottlenecks and improve performance. This initiative builds on their successful past collaborations in quantum machine learning and seeks to strengthen the quantum technology sectors in both South Korea and Canada.

feed.title: Is AMD Stock Overvalued?
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock is currently considered relatively expensive despite strong operating performance and financial status, according to a multi-factor evaluation by Trefis Team. The main concern is its "Very High valuation," with a price target of $143 suggested, leading to advice to lessen exposure. While AMD shows strong growth and moderate profitability, its resilience during economic downturns, such as the 2022 Inflation Shock and the 2008 Global Financial Crisis, has been weak compared to the S&P 500.

feed.title: JetCool Collaborates with Broadcom to Deliver Innovative Liquid Cooling for Next-Generation AI XPUs
feed.summary: JetCool, a Flex company, has partnered with Broadcom to create a production-ready liquid cooling solution for next-generation AI XPUs, addressing the multi-kilowatt power demands of advanced AI silicon. This collaboration combines JetCool's direct-to-chip cooling technology with Flex's global manufacturing capabilities to enable high-volume deployment in hyperscale AI infrastructures. The solution supports sustained multi-kilowatt ASIC operation at high heat flux levels and aims to advance scalable thermal architectures for future AI silicon generations.

feed.title: Synopsys Outlines Vision for Engineering the Future
feed.summary: Synopsys presented its vision for a new silicon-to-systems design paradigm at its Converge 2026 conference, emphasizing AI-enabled and software-defined engineering. The company unveiled new solutions, including Multiphysics Fusion technology integrating Ansys capabilities for chip design, an L4 agentic design and verification workflow powered by AgentEngineer technology, and the first major Ansys product release since its acquisition. Additionally, Synopsys introduced advancements in hardware-assisted verification and an Electronics Digital Twin platform to accelerate physical AI system development.

feed.title: TSM Soars 34% in Six Months: Is the Stock Still Worth Buying?
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM) has seen a significant 33.8% gain over the past six months, outperforming the broader tech sector due to its strong involvement in the AI boom. The company's financial performance shows substantial revenue and earnings growth driven by advanced chip demand from major AI clients. Despite the rally, TSM's valuation remains reasonable, making it an attractive investment given its leadership in chip manufacturing and continued growth prospects in AI.

feed.title: Intel has manufacturing capacity issues. They may take years to fix.
feed.summary: Intel is facing significant manufacturing capacity issues, particularly with server chips due to surging demand from data centers and emerging AI trends. While the company has redirected some production, CEO Lip-Bu Tan stated that fully meeting demand is a "multiyear journey" to rebuild capacity. Analysts are concerned about Intel's ability to capitalize on market opportunities and its current 18a node's low yields, which could impact its foundry business potential.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Enters a Multi-Year Partnership With Adeia
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) has entered a multi-year licensing agreement with Adeia, gaining access to Adeia's IP portfolio for hybrid bonding, packaging, and processing technologies. This partnership resolves previous patent disputes between the two companies. Amidst this, an analyst from Bernstein maintained a Hold rating on AMD, noting potential "extra friction" for chipmakers due to impending US regulations on AI chip exports.

feed.title: Inside Synopsys' race to test ever-bigger AI chips twice as fast
feed.summary: Synopsys announced new software-defined hardware-assisted verification (HAV) enhancements, including ZeBu Server 5 and new HAPS-200 and ZeBu-200 12 FPGA platforms, to address the escalating complexity and demand for AI chip verification. These advancements offer up to 2x performance and capacity, enabling earlier detection of bugs and faster software bring-up for AI designs from data centers to the edge. The company emphasized that its innovations support over quadrillions of verification cycles to ensure "first-time-right silicon" for advanced AI architectures.

feed.title: Synopsys Introduces Software-Defined Hardware-Assisted Verification to Enable AI Proliferation
feed.summary: Synopsys has announced significant advancements in its hardware-assisted verification (HAV) portfolio, introducing new software-defined platforms and capabilities to address the increasing complexities of AI chip verification. These innovations aim to boost performance and capacity for designing mega AI chips, improving test automation, and enabling faster detection of bugs. The new HAPS-200 12 FPGA and ZeBu-200 12 FPGA platforms offer higher capacity and performance for diverse AI applications, emphasizing the need for scaled verification in the rapidly evolving AI landscape.

feed.title: Synopsys Outlines Vision for Engineering the Future
feed.summary: Synopsys, Inc. unveiled its vision for engineering the future at Synopsys Converge 2026, focusing on a new silicon-to-systems design paradigm for AI-powered products. The company announced innovative solutions, including Multiphysics Fusion technology integrating Synopsys and Ansys tools, new AgentEngineer AI-powered design and verification workflows, the first major Ansys product release under Synopsys with AI-driven simulation, and advanced hardware-assisted verification platforms. These advancements aim to accelerate product innovation, improve design accuracy, and enhance productivity for engineers developing next-generation intelligent systems.

feed.title: Synopsys rolls out new software tools for designing AI chips
feed.summary: Synopsys has launched new software tools to address the complexities of designing AI chips, marking its first major offering since acquiring Ansys. These tools aim to integrate mechanical engineering considerations, such as heat management in "chiplet" designs, directly into the chip design process, ultimately reducing costs and improving performance. CEO Sassine Ghazi stated that this integration helps overcome siloed design processes, enhancing efficiency and product potential.

feed.title: AMD Stock Just Flipped the Script: What Smart Money Is Seeing Now
feed.summary: AMD is trending again due to its focus on AI chips, growth potential, and strategic positioning in the AI data center, gaming, and PC markets. The company's MI300 AI chips are competing with Nvidia, while its Ryzen CPUs and Radeon GPUs continue to attract gamers and PC builders. Despite high expectations and market volatility, AMD presents a high-risk, high-potential investment opportunity driven by its ongoing efforts in AI and its strong presence in the consumer market.

feed.title: Micron Applied Materials Tie-Up Puts AI Memory Growth And Valuation In Focus
feed.summary: Micron Technology and Applied Materials have partnered to advance AI memory solutions through a new Equipment and Process Innovation and Commercialization (EPIC) Center in Silicon Valley. This collaboration, focusing on DRAM, HBM, and NAND technologies, aims to accelerate the commercialization of AI-focused memory products. While Micron's shares show significant recent gains, Simply Wall St assesses the stock as materially overvalued, highlighting pricing risks if AI infrastructure sentiment cools or R&D benefits are delayed.

feed.title: QCOM Chips to Power AI Robotics Capabilities: Will it Boost Prospects?
feed.summary: Qualcomm (QCOM) has partnered with NEURA Robotics to integrate its robotics processors, AI acceleration, and software into NEURA's humanoid robots, aiming for widespread industrial applications. This collaboration positions Qualcomm to expand its AI chip presence in the growing robotics market, though it faces strong competition from companies like NVIDIA and AMD. Despite this strategic move, Qualcomm's stock has underperformed the industry, and its earnings estimates for fiscal years 2026 and 2027 have seen declines.

feed.title: Merit Medical Systems Stock To $90?
feed.summary: Merit Medical Systems (MMSI) stock has recently fallen by 16% in less than a month, prompting an analysis of its potential for recovery. Historically, MMSI has shown a strong tendency to rebound post-dips, with a median 12-month return of 34% after similar sharp declines. The company also passes basic financial quality checks, suggesting that the current dip might present a buying opportunity with a target price of $90.

feed.title: Applied Materials and SK Hynix to co-develop next-generation DRAM and HBM at $5bn Silicon Valley R&D facility
feed.summary: Applied Materials and SK Hynix are collaborating to develop next-generation DRAM and high-bandwidth memory (HBM) at Applied's $5 billion EPIC Center in Silicon Valley. This partnership aims to boost the performance and manufacturability of future memory architectures, focusing on material innovation, process integration, and 3D advanced packaging. The initiative addresses the growing demand for energy-efficient memory technologies driven by AI systems and seeks to mitigate the ongoing memory chip shortage.

feed.title: Synopsys Unveils AI-Driven Product Innovation Solutions at Converge 2026
feed.summary: At Converge 2026, Synopsys introduced a new silicon-to-systems design paradigm integrating AI and software-defined technologies to boost R&D efficiency and market responsiveness. Key innovations include Multiphysics Fusion technology for enhanced chip design accuracy, AgentEngineer technology for accelerated design time, and an Electronics Digital Twin platform allowing up to 90% software validation before hardware availability. These solutions aim to accelerate intelligent system development and support rapid innovation in fields like automotive design.

feed.title: Synopsys AI Chip Design Tools for Chiplet Complexity | 2026 - News and Statistics
feed.summary: Synopsys has launched new AI chip design software, the first major product release since its acquisition of Ansys. These tools aim to tackle the increasing complexity of AI chip designs, particularly those using multiple chiplets, by integrating capabilities to manage challenges like heat-induced warping directly into the design process. CEO Sassine Ghazi stated that this integrated approach will lead to better performance, lower power consumption, and reduced costs by addressing these factors earlier.

feed.title: 10 Information Technology Stocks Whale Activity In Today's Session
feed.summary: This article identifies significant "whale" activity in the options market for ten Information Technology stocks, including Oracle, Micron Technology, and NVIDIA. It details specific bullish or bearish option trades, such as call and put contracts, along with their expiration dates, strike prices, and total trade values. The presence of large-scale options activity is highlighted as a potential indicator for traders searching for future opportunities.

feed.title: BlackBerry Boosts Automotive And Industrial Adoption With QNX Learning Initiative
feed.summary: BlackBerry is expanding its QNX online learning platform to accelerate developer readiness, aiming to strengthen its position in the automotive and industrial sectors. This initiative, which includes support for AMD Ryzen Embedded processors, seeks to address the shortage of skilled embedded software engineers and bolster BlackBerry's market presence. Despite a mixed technical outlook for its stock, the company anticipates an increase in EPS and revenue for its upcoming financial update.

feed.title: Hyperscale Data Provides 2026 Revenue Guidance of $180 Million to $200 Million as Artificial Intelligence Infrastructure Scale-Up Continues and Higher Margin Platforms Expand
feed.summary: Hyperscale Data anticipates record revenue in fiscal year 2026, projected to be between $180 million and $200 million, representing 80%-100% year-over-year growth. This growth is attributed to expanded AI infrastructure, software offerings, blockchain, and finance operations, including a full-year contribution from the reconsolidated Ballista Group. The company is also targeting profitability in the fourth quarter of 2026 due to an evolving revenue mix focused on higher-margin platforms.

feed.title: Guarantor: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC is offering $3,073,000 Digital Barrier Notes linked to the common stock of Advanced Micro Devices, Inc., due September 14, 2027. These notes are fully and unconditionally guaranteed by JPMorgan Chase & Co. The notes are designed for investors seeking a fixed return of 31.00% at maturity if AMD's stock price at maturity is greater than or equal to 60.00% of its initial value, but investors risk losing a significant portion or all of their principal if the stock performance is unfavorable.

feed.title: Adeia, Advanced Micro Devices Settle Patent Infringement Dispute
feed.summary: Adeia and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) have settled two patent infringement lawsuits. Adeia had alleged that AMD's products, including Ryzen processors and Instinct accelerators, infringed its semiconductor bonding technologies patents. The terms of the settlement, which led to the dismissal of the suits filed in November 2025, were not disclosed.

feed.title: Adeia, Advanced Micro Devices Settle Patent Infringement Dispute
feed.summary: Adeia Semiconductor Bonding Technologies Inc. and Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) have settled two patent infringement lawsuits. The suits, filed by Adeia in November 2025, accused AMD of infringing patents with its Ryzen desktop processors and Instinct accelerator products. The terms of the settlement, which led to the dismissal of the cases by Judge David Counts on March 10, were not disclosed.

feed.title: Adeia, Advanced Micro Devices Settle Patent Infringement Dispute
feed.summary: Adeia Semiconductor Bonding Technologies Inc. and Advanced Micro Devices Inc. have settled two patent infringement lawsuits. The suits, filed by Adeia in November 2025, accused AMD of infringing patents with its Ryzen processors and Instinct accelerator product lines. The terms of the settlement were not disclosed.

feed.title: 3 Forces That Could Shake Advanced Micro Devices Stock
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock faces three significant risks: broadening AI chip export controls, margin erosion from patent litigation and royalties, and a potential collapse of its gaming segment due to market share loss to Nvidia. Historically, AMD's stock has experienced sharp declines during market downturns and even during milder corrections. The article highlights these risks by providing evidence and potential timelines for their impact, while also noting current financial performance metrics like revenue growth, cash generation, and valuation.

feed.title: Frequency Electronics Q3 2026 Earnings: $1.6M Profit on $16.9M Revenue - News and Statistics
feed.summary: Frequency Electronics (FEIM) has reported a net income of $1.6 million for its fiscal third quarter of 2026, translating to earnings of sixteen cents per share. The company, which specializes in high-accuracy timing systems, also generated $16.9 million in total revenue during the same period. This announcement provides key financial insights for investors and stakeholders.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Arista Networks (ANET) After Its Strong Multi Year Rally?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Arista Networks (ANET) after a significant multi-year rally to determine if it's still a good investment. Using a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model and Price/Earnings (P/E) ratio, the analysis suggests ANET is fairly valued by DCF but overvalued by P/E when compared to its "Fair Ratio." The article also presents bull and bear narratives for Arista Networks, illustrating how different growth and margin assumptions lead to varying fair value estimations.

feed.title: Assessing Flex (FLEX) Valuation After Expanded AMD AI Collaboration And Mexico Manufacturing Growth
feed.summary: Flex (FLEX) is receiving renewed attention after expanding its collaboration with AMD on AI platforms and increasing manufacturing capacity in Mexico, which enhances its electronics production capabilities. Despite a 4.07% 1-day share price gain, its 30-day return declined by 4.06%, though longer-term returns are significant. Analysts present a mixed valuation, with one narrative suggesting Flex is overvalued at $63.39 compared to a fair value of $50.97, while another highlights its P/E ratio is lower than peers and industry averages, indicating a potentially more forgiving valuation.

feed.title: Nio sees EV costs rising on memory chip shortage and metal price rebound
feed.summary: Nio expects electric vehicle production costs to rise by nearly 10,000 yuan ($1,460) per premium EV in 2026 due to increasing prices of memory chips and raw materials. Despite this pressure, the company plans to absorb these costs without adjusting retail prices, leveraging its existing pricing system and maintaining its full-year non-GAAP operating profit target for 2026. Nio aims to counteract long-term cost pressures through technological innovation such as lightweighting and investments in integrated die-casting and aluminum alloy materials.

feed.title: Applied Materials, SK Hynix Expand R&D Partnership for Next-Generation DRAM
feed.summary: Applied Materials and SK Hynix have signed a long-term R&D agreement to accelerate the development of next-generation DRAM and high-bandwidth memory (HBM). This collaboration will involve exploring new semiconductor materials, integrated process technologies, and 3D packaging, with engineers conducting R&D at Applied's EPIC Center in Silicon Valley and SK Hynix utilizing Applied's advanced packaging R&D center in Singapore. The partnership aims to improve performance and manufacturability of future memory architectures, particularly for AI applications, by adopting a parallel chip development model.
feed.source: THE ELEC, Korea Electronics Industry Media
feed.source_domain: THE ELEC, Korea Electronics Industry Media

feed.title: Assessing Himax Technologies (HIMX) Valuation As Embedded World 2026 Showcases AI Endpoint And Imaging Advances
feed.summary: Himax Technologies (HIMX) is showcasing its AI endpoint solutions and imaging advances at Embedded World 2026, following a recent surge in its share price. Despite positive short-term performance and revenue growth, analysts value Himax at $8.54, suggesting it is currently overvalued compared to its latest close of $9.15. However, when considering its P/E ratio of 36.4x, Himax appears to trade at a discount to the US Semiconductor industry (41.9x) and its peers (70.5x), indicating potential future earnings upside.

feed.title: From Dependence to Control. Meta’s Development of Proprietary AI Chips
feed.summary: Meta Platforms is strategically developing its own AI chips, codenamed MTIA, with plans for four generations by late 2027 to reduce dependence on external suppliers like Nvidia and AMD. This initiative aims to optimize operating costs for data centers, enhance control over computing infrastructure, and support the development of AI-powered products. While full independence will take time, this move signals Meta's commitment to improving financial performance and competitive positioning in the long term.

feed.title: HubSpot vs. Intel: Which AI-Linked Tech Stock is the Better Bet Now?
feed.summary: This article compares HubSpot (HUBS) and Intel (INTC), two tech companies heavily integrating AI into their strategies. HubSpot is focusing on AI to enrich customer data and enhance its product suite, while Intel is shifting towards AI and data-centric businesses amid declining PC reliance. The analysis concludes that HubSpot, with its superior Zacks Rank and stronger growth estimates, appears to be the better investment option despite Intel's recent price performance gains.

feed.title: Intel Launches Core Series 2 Processor with Real-Time Performance and Expands Edge AI Portfolio
feed.summary: Intel has launched its Core Series 2 processor with P-cores, an industrial-ready platform designed for mission-critical edge applications requiring deterministic performance. Concurrently, Intel unveiled its latest Edge AI suite for Health & Life Sciences, offering validated reference pipelines for AI-powered patient monitoring solutions. This initiative expands Intel's comprehensive edge computing portfolio, addressing diverse customer needs from industrial control to advanced healthcare AI.

feed.title: Arista Networks Unveils 12.8Tbps Optical Module for AI Networking
feed.summary: Arista Networks has introduced its new XPO optical module, designed to meet the high demands of AI networking with an impressive 12.8Tbps throughput per module. This module features liquid cooling for stable operation under high loads and quadruples rack density compared to traditional optics. It also supports industry standards and is set to debut at the 2026 OFC exhibition.

feed.title: Connor Clark & Lunn Investment Management Ltd. Buys 307,223 Shares of Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. $AMD
feed.summary: Connor Clark & Lunn Investment Management Ltd. significantly increased its stake in Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) by 55.5% in the third quarter, acquiring 307,223 additional shares for a total holding worth $139.2 million. This comes as AMD reported a strong Q4 earnings beat, surpassing analyst estimates for both EPS and revenue. Despite positive analyst ratings and strategic partnerships, concerns regarding insider selling, potential customer risk from Meta's in-house AI chip development, and valuation debates persist.

feed.title: Silicon Motion Launches SM8008 PCIe Gen 5 Controller for Enterprise Boot Drive and Ultra-Low-Power Storage
feed.summary: Silicon Motion has launched the SM8008, a PCIe Gen 5 x4 NVMe enterprise SSD controller designed for data center boot drives and power-sensitive enterprise storage. Built on TSMC 6 nm process technology, it offers power efficiency, high performance up to 14 GB/s sequential throughput, and enterprise-grade security features. The controller supports multiple industry-standard form factors and meets OCP Hyperscale NVMe Boot SSD specifications, addressing the growing demand for reliable boot storage in expanding AI and cloud infrastructure.

feed.title: Rep. Gilbert Ray Cisneros, Jr. Buys Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (NASDAQ:AMD) Stock
feed.summary: Representative Gilbert Ray Cisneros, Jr. (Democratic-California) recently purchased between $1,001 and $15,000 worth of Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock on February 10th. feed.title: Oracle Earnings Heated Up Its Stock—But Couldn't Spark an AI Rally
feed.summary: Oracle's stock rose significantly after exceeding earnings estimates and increasing revenue guidance, driven by strong AI demand and a growing backlog. However, this positive news for Oracle and semiconductor stocks failed to lift the broader market or most other AI-related companies, which faced headwinds from rising oil prices and geopolitical risks. The report suggests that while AI demand is broad, market-wide AI optimism is being tempered by broader economic concerns.

feed.title: TSMC Supply Chain Risks Rise As Valuation Premium Faces New Test
feed.summary: Ongoing conflict in Iran has raised new concerns about global semiconductor supply chains and potential disruptions for TSMC, a major contract chip manufacturer. While TSMC reports no immediate operational impact, prolonged conflict could affect key inputs and logistics, posing additional risks for electronics and AI hardware makers. This geopolitical tension adds to existing concerns about chip production concentration and TSMC's ability to maintain operations, further scrutinizing its valuation which currently trades 28.9% above Simply Wall St's fair value estimate.

feed.title: New SSD controller delivers Gen5 speed under 5W for AI data centers
feed.summary: Silicon Motion has launched the SM8008, the industry's first purpose-built PCIe Gen5 controller designed for enterprise boot SSDs and ultra-low-power storage. This new controller offers speeds up to 14GB/s and over 2.3 million 4K IOPS, while consuming less than 5 watts of active power. Built on TSMC's 6nm process, the SM8008 meets OCP Hyperscale NVMe Boot SSD specifications and includes robust enterprise-grade security features, addressing the growing demand for efficient and secure storage in AI and cloud data centers.

feed.title: Barclays (ATMP) offers auto‑callable $1,000 notes tied to AMD, AVGO, MRVL
feed.summary: Barclays Bank PLC is offering auto-callable, market-linked securities with a $1,000 principal amount, tied to the performance of Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD), Broadcom Inc. (AVGO), and Marvell Technology, Inc. (MRVL) stocks. These notes offer a monthly contingent coupon of at least 21.00% per annum, conditional on the lowest-performing stock meeting a threshold, and can be automatically called if the lowest-performing stock reaches its starting price. Investors face principal loss if the securities are not called and the lowest-performing stock falls below 50% of its starting price at maturity, and are also subject to Barclays' credit risk and potential U.K. Bail-in Power.

feed.title: UPDATE -- Tidal Financial Group and Defiance ETFs Announce Reverse Stock Splits for Select ETFs
feed.summary: Tidal Financial Group and Defiance ETFs have announced reverse stock splits for twenty-four of Defiance's exchange-traded funds (ETFs) to reduce outstanding shares and proportionally increase share prices without affecting total investment value. Shareholders of record on specified dates will have their shares combined according to varying split ratios, with the process handled automatically by DTC. Fractional shares may result in cash compensation with potential tax implications, and investors are advised to consult tax advisors.

feed.title: Synopsys Introduces Software-Defined Hardware-Assisted Verification to Enable AI Proliferation
feed.summary: Synopsys has announced advancements in its hardware-assisted verification (HAV) portfolio, including new hardware platforms and capabilities, to meet the growing demand for AI chip verification from data centers to the edge. These software-defined HAV solutions offer enhanced performance, scalability, and test automation, enabling faster and more comprehensive verification of complex multi-die and AI chips. The new platforms, like HAPS-200 12 FPGA and ZeBu-200 12 FPGA, are designed to accelerate system-level verification and validation, supporting pre-silicon development across various industries.

Despite some positive long-term developments like AI infrastructure partnerships and strong demand for AI GPUs, negative sentiment currently overshadows these factors.

feed.title: Intel Global Channel Chief On CPU Shortage: ‘Everyone Is Impacted’
feed.summary: Intel's Global Channel Chief, Dave Guzzi, stated that the company's CPU supply constraints are impacting partners across the board, including cloud service providers, OEMs, and builders, though any price increases are expected to be minor compared to memory chips. The shortage is driven by high demand from hyperscalers and AI data centers, as well as PC sales using older generation processors. Intel anticipates the supply constraints to peak in Q1, with gradual improvement throughout the year, and is prioritizing server CPUs and high-end PC processors.

feed.title: Xanadu to merge with Crane Harbor (NASDAQ: CHAC) in $500M deal
feed.summary: Xanadu Quantum Technologies and Crane Harbor Acquisition Corp. (NASDAQ: CHAC) announced a proposed business combination to form a new entity, NewCo, expected to be capitalized with approximately $500 million. This amount includes $225 million from Crane Harbor's trust account and $275 million from committed private placement investors, assuming no redemptions. The merger aims to list NewCo on both Nasdaq and the Toronto Stock Exchange, with the Registration Statement on Form F-4 having been declared effective by the SEC on February 27, 2026.

feed.title: Stock Market Today Review: SPY, QQQ Tumble as Oil Approaches $100 on Iranian Leader’s Vow to Keep Hormuz Closed
feed.summary: The S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) both fell over 1.5% as the U.S.-Iran conflict escalated, with Iran's leader threatening to keep the Strait of Hormuz closed and targeting U.S. bases. This led to a 10% surge in crude oil prices, reaching $95, making energy the best-performing sector while industrial stocks suffered from increased operating costs and global trade uncertainties. The market also reacted to new U.S. trade investigations against 16 countries and a significant drop in the U.S. trade deficit.
