The company also expanded its Ryzen AI portfolio with new Ryzen AI 400 Series processors for next-gen AI PCs.

feed.title: Everspin unveils unified MRAM architecture for next‑generation embedded systems
feed.summary: Everspin Technologies has introduced UNISYST, a new family of magnetoresistive random-access memory (MRAM) devices. This unified architecture aims to replace traditional NOR flash in high-performance embedded systems by providing a single solution for both code and data storage. UNISYST offers significant performance improvements over NOR flash, including faster read/write speeds and higher write endurance, addressing the limitations of existing non-volatile memory technologies in demanding applications such as edge AI, automotive, and aerospace.

feed.title: MaxLinear to Showcase Next‑Generation 1.6T Rushmore DSP Live at OFC 2026
feed.summary: MaxLinear, Inc. will showcase its next-generation 1.6T PHY chipset, including the Rushmore 200G/lane PAM-4 DSP and Washington 200G/lane TIA, live at OFC 2026. Demonstrations will highlight the Rushmore DSP's performance in optical and electrical interfaces, supporting AI data center connectivity needs. The company's comprehensive portfolio addresses both scale-out and scale-up networking architectures, enabling current 400G and 800G deployments and future 1.6T systems.

feed.title: Taiwan's Foundry Model: The Global Chip Industry's Central Hub in 2026 - News and Statistics
feed.summary: As of early 2026, Taiwan remains the central hub for advanced semiconductor manufacturing, dominating the production of sophisticated logic chips crucial for AI systems and military hardware. This dominance, originating from a strategic shift decades ago with the pure-play foundry model, makes Taiwan an indispensable economic node but also a potential single point of failure for global supply chains. While the island faces physical constraints like resource intensity and skilled labor shortages, and geopolitical risks, TSMC is diversifying production geographically, though the most advanced research and manufacturing will stay in Taiwan.

feed.title: US Commerce Dept Reportedly Withdraws Planned Rule On AI Chip Exports
feed.summary: The U.S. Department of Commerce has reportedly withdrawn a proposed rule on AI chip exports, signaling a shift in the nation's strategy for regulating the global AI chip market. feed.title: Synopsys Software-defined Hardware-assisted Verification Enables AI Proliferation

feed.title: AMD and Celestica Announce Collaboration to Advance the Next Era of AI with “Helios” Rack-Scale AI Platform

feed.title: Moody's economist Mark Zandi on thousands of layoffs at Jack Dorsey's Block; If companies cut workers as Block did, outright job losses appear to be ...

He warns that if other companies follow Block's lead, especially given the market's positive reaction to the layoffs, outright job losses are increasingly likely.

feed.title: MaxLinear Unveils Annapurna 224G Scale-Up Retimer to Extend Copper Connectivity in AI Data Centers
feed.summary: MaxLinear has launched Annapurna, a 224G scale-up retimer designed to enhance copper connectivity in AI data centers, supporting up to 1.6Tbps of electrical connectivity. This technology enables copper backplanes and Active Electrical Cables (AECs) to reliably operate at 224Gbps per lane using PAM4 signaling, offering significant power, latency, and cost benefits compared to optical solutions for scale-up networking within and between racks. Annapurna features advanced DSP algorithms to overcome channel impairments, ensuring robust electrical connectivity over distances that previously required optical interconnects.

feed.title: HIVE Digital Technologies subsidiary BUZZ expands AI data center capacity in Canada
feed.summary: HIVE Digital Technologies, through its subsidiary BUZZ High Performance Computing, is significantly expanding its AI data center capacity in Canada, increasing its liquid-cooled data center footprint fourfold from 4 MW to 16.6 MW. This expansion, a partnership with Bell Canada, involves a new colocation facility in British Columbia and aims to deploy over 6,000 next-generation AI-optimized GPUs. The company is targeting $200 million in contracted annualized run-rate revenue from high-performance computing by March 31, 2027, as it pivots its focus towards AI cloud services and away from traditional cryptocurrency mining.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Stock Drops As Insiders Cash Out $33 Million
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) reported strong Q4 earnings, beating expectations with impressive revenue growth and a positive outlook for its data center segment. Despite institutional buying, the stock is trading below key moving averages, and insiders have sold approximately $33.1 million worth of shares. Competitive pressures from a new Chinese GPU maker and Meta's in-house chip development also pose potential headwinds for AMD.

feed.title: A Look At Public Service Enterprise Group’s Valuation As It Moves To Eliminate Supermajority Voting Requirements
feed.summary: Public Service Enterprise Group (PEG) is proposing to eliminate supermajority voting requirements, a governance change that could impact shareholder value. The company's stock is currently trading at $83.73, considered modestly undervalued based on fair value estimates but slightly expensive by a Discounted Cash Flow model. Future demand for electricity, especially from data centers, and regulatory decisions on grid investment will be key factors influencing PEG's valuation.

feed.title: Adeia Inc stock hits 52-week high at 23.63 USD
feed.summary: Adeia Inc. (ADEA) has reached a new 52-week high of $23.52, with shares currently trading at $23.56, reflecting a significant 68.62% 1-year total return. The company's valuation is considered attractive with a PEG ratio of 0.3. This growth is bolstered by recent multi-year licensing agreements with Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and United Microelectronics Corporation (UMC), leading to increased price targets from Roth/MKM and Rosenblatt.

feed.title: The AI Pipeline: A Deep-Dive into Credo Technology Group (CRDO) and the Future of 1.6T Connectivity
feed.summary: Credo Technology Group (CRDO) is highlighted as a crucial player in the AI infrastructure "plumbing," specializing in high-speed connectivity solutions like Active Electrical Cables (AECs) and optical DSPs. The company has seen explosive growth and stock performance, driven by the demand for 800G and upcoming 1.6T networking in hyperscale data centers. Despite risks like customer concentration, Credo's focus on low-power, high-speed data transmission positions it as a standard-setter in the evolving AI era.

feed.title: 20,700 Shares in Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Ltd. $TSM Purchased by First Trust Bank Ltd.
feed.summary: First Trust Bank Ltd. recently acquired 20,700 shares of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Ltd. (TSM) for approximately $5.78 million, making it their fourth-largest holding and 13.2% of their portfolio. The semiconductor giant commands about 70% of the global foundry market and recently reported strong quarterly results with a $1.75 trillion market cap. Analysts widely recommend a "Buy" rating for TSM, with a consensus price target of $391.43, despite current geopolitical risks affecting foreign markets.

feed.title: Comfort Systems USA, Inc. $FIX Shares Purchased by Granahan Investment Management LLC
feed.summary: Granahan Investment Management LLC increased its stake in Comfort Systems USA (NYSE: FIX) by 35.2% during the third quarter, acquiring an additional 24,160 shares and bringing their total ownership to 92,772 shares valued at $76.55 million. This makes Comfort Systems USA the 6th largest position in Granahan's portfolio. Other institutional investors also adjusted their holdings, and analysts currently rate the stock as a "Buy" with an average target price of $1,573.67.

feed.title: Intel Corporation Stock (ISIN: US4581401001) Consolidates Near $45 Amid AI Push and Foundry Challenges
feed.summary: Intel Corporation's stock is consolidating around $45, reflecting a balance between optimism for its AI innovations and ongoing losses in its foundry segment. The company is forging strategic alliances in AI and 6G, with its foundry business projected to break even by 2027. Despite a strong recovery in 2025, analysts mostly rate the stock as "hold" due to stretched valuations and execution risks, though long-term growth is anticipated.

feed.title: Akamai Technologies (AKAM) Is Up 5.4% After $200 Million AI Cloud Deal With Major Tech Client
feed.summary: Akamai Technologies saw its stock rise by 5.4% following the announcement of a four-year, $200 million AI services agreement with a major U.S. tech company. This deal involves deploying one of the world's largest NVIDIA Blackwell RTX PRO 6000 Server Edition GPU clusters on its cloud platform, reinforcing Akamai's focus on cloud and AI growth. However, the company also faces internal debate regarding political spending transparency and potential risks like increased CapEx and customer concentration.

feed.title: Hilltop Holdings Inc. Has $4.53 Million Stock Position in Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Ltd. $TSM
feed.summary: Hilltop Holdings Inc. recently increased its stake in Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Ltd. (NYSE: TSM) by 11.8% in Q3, now holding 16,206 shares valued at approximately $4.53 million. TSMC, which commands around 70% of the global foundry market, reported strong financials with $3.11 EPS and $30.65 billion in revenue last quarter. Analysts maintain a broadly positive outlook with a consensus "Buy" rating and a target price of $391.43, with Barclays recently raising its target to $450.

feed.title: Insight Holdings Group LLC Has $59.50 Million Stake in Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Ltd. $TSM
feed.summary: Insight Holdings Group LLC has trimmed its stake in Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Ltd. (TSM) by 13.7%, valuing its remaining 213,030 shares at $59.50 million, making TSM its 8th largest holding. Despite this reduction, TSM maintains a dominant position in the global foundry market, strong financials, and a "Buy" consensus rating with an average target price of $391.43. The company also announced a quarterly dividend of $0.9503 per share, yielding approximately 1.1%.

feed.title: KADENSA CAPITAL Ltd Has $47.30 Million Stake in Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Ltd. $TSM
feed.summary: KADENSA CAPITAL Ltd significantly increased its stake in Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Ltd. (TSM) by 418.2% in Q3, now holding 169,353 shares valued at $47.30 million. This makes TSM the firm's fifth-largest holding, representing about 4.9% of its portfolio. The semiconductor giant shows strong market performance with robust earnings, a $1.75 trillion market cap, and a dominant position in the global foundry market, leading to a "Buy" consensus rating from analysts with a target price of $391.43.

feed.title: Inside "Helios": Celestica and AMD build AI racks for cloud, enterprise by 2026
feed.summary: Celestica (TSX: CLS) and AMD (NASDAQ: AMD) have announced a strategic collaboration to launch the "Helios" rack-scale AI platform, targeting cloud, enterprise, and research environments by late 2026. Celestica will be responsible for the R&D, design, and manufacturing of scale-up networking switches that are optimized for AMD Instinct MI450 Series GPUs and will utilize the UALoE architecture. The partnership aims to deliver high-performance AI infrastructure quickly and efficiently, leveraging both companies' expertise to meet the growing demand for large-scale AI deployments.

feed.title: Iron Triangle Partners LP Purchases New Position in Veracyte, Inc. $VCYT
feed.summary: Iron Triangle Partners LP has acquired a new position in Veracyte, Inc. ($VCYT), purchasing 485,000 shares valued at approximately $16.65 million. This investment makes Veracyte the fund's 21st largest holding, representing about 2.4% of its portfolio. Despite recent insider selling, with 86,521 shares offloaded by company insiders, Veracyte reported better-than-expected earnings for the latest quarter.

feed.title: Coca-Cola named Top Pick in beverages at Morgan Stanley
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has reinstated Coca-Cola (KO) as its Top Pick in the beverage sector and named it the overall Top Pick in North American consumer staples. The firm prefers beverages due to better pricing power and innovation contributions, especially with anticipated lingering high inflation from the Iran conflict. Morgan Stanley maintains an Overweight rating on Coca-Cola shares with an $87 price target.

feed.title: Lightwave Logic High-Speed Modulator Platform Now Available in GDS Factory PDK for GlobalFoundries Silicon Photonics Platform
feed.summary: Lightwave Logic's high-speed modulator platform, based on proprietary electro-optic polymers, is now integrated into the GDSFactory process design kit (PDK) for GlobalFoundries' silicon photonics manufacturing platform. This integration allows customers to incorporate Lightwave Logic's polymer-based modulators directly into their photonic integrated circuit designs, facilitating the development of high-speed, low-power solutions for data centers and AI acceleration. The collaboration includes validation tape-outs and GlobalFoundries' optimization of its silicon photonics process to support Lightwave Logic's advanced device architecture.

feed.title: Lam Research (LCRX) Gained From Strong Demand Uptick
feed.summary: Lam Research Corporation (LRCX) was a leading contributor to Artisan Value Fund's outperformance, as highlighted in their fourth-quarter 2025 investor letter. The company, a leader in wafer fabrication equipment, is experiencing strong demand for its chipmaking tools due to the massive buildout of data centers running AI applications. Artisan Value Fund purchased LRCX shares when they were undervalued, recognizing the company's solid financial condition and attractive valuation.

feed.title: Celestica, AMD partner for ‘Helios’ rack-scale AI platform
feed.summary: Celestica Inc. (CLS) and AMD have formed a strategic partnership to launch the "Helios" rack-scale AI platform. This collaboration combines AMD's computing leadership with Celestica's expertise in networking switch technologies, focusing on the R&D, design, and manufacturing of scale-up networking switches for the platform. The "Helios" platform, based on Open Compute Project standards, is expected to be available to customers in late 2026, utilizing advanced networking silicon to interconnect next-generation AMD Instinct MI450 Series GPUs for large-scale AI clusters.

feed.title: Intel Corp Stock (INTC) Opened Up by 6.90% on Mar 16: Facts Behind the Movement
feed.summary: Intel Corp (INTC) stock rose by 6.90% due to positive developments in its AI strategy and advancements in its foundry business. The company launched Core Ultra Series 3 processors for AI PCs and has solid progress with its 18A process node, securing major customers like Microsoft and Amazon Web Services for its foundry services. Despite intraday volatility and ongoing losses in its foundry division, the overall semiconductor market recovery and increasing AI demand provide a positive outlook for Intel.

feed.title: AI-Driven Memory Chip Demand to Drive Micron's DRAM Revenue in Q2
feed.summary: Micron Technology (MU) is expected to see significant growth in its DRAM revenue for the second fiscal quarter of 2026, driven by high demand for memory chips in AI systems. The company's strong partnerships with tech giants like NVIDIA and Advanced Micro Devices for its HBM3E memory products are contributing to this growth. Analysts anticipate a 137.8% year-over-year increase in revenues and a 457.1% rise in non-GAAP earnings per share for the quarter.

feed.title: Roth/MKM raises Adeia stock price target on AMD licensing deal
feed.summary: Roth/MKM has increased its price target for Adeia Inc. (NASDAQ:ADEA) shares to $34 from $27, maintaining a Buy rating, following Adeia's multi-year licensing agreement with AMD. This deal validates hybrid bonding technology and resolves prior litigation concerns, with Adeia's stock trading near its 52-week high after a 69% return over the past year. Other analysts like Rosenblatt (to $40) and BWS Financial (to $30) have also raised their price targets on Adeia following recent licensing agreements and litigation settlements.

feed.title: Why This Analyst Is Still Pounding the Table on AMD Stock
feed.summary: BNP Paribas analyst David O'Connor is highly bullish on AMD stock, maintaining an "Outperform" rating and a $265 price target after a meeting with AMD executives. This optimism stems from AMD's significant artificial intelligence infrastructure partnerships, including multi-billion-dollar deals with Meta Platforms and OpenAI for Instinct GPUs. Additionally, O'Connor sees strong growth potential in AMD's EPYC server CPU business, expecting further gigawatt-scale deals and increasing demand for its next-generation processors.

feed.title: Intel's roadmaps examined — 14A, Nova Lake, Diamond Rapids & AI accelerator push
feed.summary: This article examines Intel's comprehensive roadmap, detailing upcoming CPU architectures like Nova Lake and Diamond Rapids, alongside its ambitious AI accelerator strategy, including Crescent Island and Jaguar Shores. It highlights Intel's critical push to scale its 18A process node and secure external foundry customers for the 14A node, emphasizing the simultaneous need for manufacturing and product launch success. The article also delves into the strategic shift in processor designs, moving from monolithic dies to multi-tile architectures and the evolution of Intel's process nodes (Intel 4, Intel 3, 20A, 18A, 14A).

feed.title: AI-Driven Memory Chip Demand to Drive Micron's DRAM Revenue in Q2
feed.summary: Micron Technology (MU) is expected to see significant growth in its second-quarter fiscal 2026 revenue, driven by escalating demand for memory chips in AI applications. The company's DRAM segment, fueled by strong ties with tech giants like NVIDIA and AMD, is projected to be a major contributor, with estimated year-over-year growth of 145.5%. Micron's mass production of HBM3E memory for NVIDIA's AI chips further solidifies its position in the high-performance computing market.

feed.title: SuperMicro takes on server leaders as AMD pushes on-premise AI
feed.summary: SuperMicro has seen significant growth in the server market, specifically in AI-related infrastructure, pushing past rivals like Lenovo and HPE to become the second-largest PC server maker behind Dell. This shift is occurring as AMD introduces "Agent Computers" designed for on-premise agentic AI workloads, aiming to compete with cloud-based AI solutions. The market is experiencing price volatility for components like GPUs, DRAM, and SSDs due to high demand, which is expected to continue impacting market dynamics in 2026.

feed.title: Celestica-AMD Partner For AI Rack-Scale Platform Deal
feed.summary: Celestica and AMD have announced a strategic partnership to advance AMD's "Helios" rack-scale AI platform. Celestica will handle the R&D, design, and manufacturing of scale-up networking switches for the "Helios" architecture, which is based on Open Compute Project standards. This collaboration aims to enhance AI solution deployments across various sectors, optimize performance for large-scale AI clusters, and reflects Celestica's focus on the growing AI market.

feed.title: EWT ETF聚焦台灣半導體 近一年報酬達50% 退休族群也關注
feed.summary: iShares MSCI 台灣 ETF (EWT) 憑藉對台灣科技產業的集中投資，特別是台積電，在過去一年中取得了50%的報酬率，吸引了投資者的關注。然而，文章也指出EWT存在集中度高、地緣政治風險及貨幣波動等問題，並與表現更佳的VanEck半導體ETF (SMH) 進行了比較，提醒投資者在享受高報酬的同時，也需審慎評估其風險。

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc (AMD) Shares Up 3.3% on Mar 16
feed.summary: Shares of Advanced Micro Devices Inc (AMD) rose 3.30% in mid-day trading on March 16, closing at $199.77. Wall Street analysts and GuruFocus estimates predict a significant upside for AMD, with an average target price of $280.14 and a GF Value of $282.73, both indicating an "Outperform" status. The stock's performance places it 25.20% below its 52-week high and 161.21% above its 52-week low.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc Stock (AMD) Moved Up by 3.15% on Mar 16: A Full Analysis
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc (AMD) stock rose by 3.15% on March 16, driven by positive analyst ratings, strategic partnerships, and a favorable policy shift regarding AI chip exports. Analysts maintain "Buy" ratings with high price targets, and AMD's collaboration with Celestica for the "Helios" AI platform and secured deals with hyperscalers like Meta Platforms and OpenAI are significant catalysts. Despite competitive pressures from NVIDIA and some insider selling, strong demand for its AI accelerators and server CPUs contributes to a bullish outlook.

feed.title: AMD Stock Outlook: Here’s Why It Could Deliver 32% Upside in 2026
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is positioned for a significant upside in its stock value, potentially delivering a 32% annualized internal rate of return through 2030, driven by its robust Data Center segment. The company has secured landmark deals for GPU deployments with Meta and OpenAI, and plans to launch next-generation AI accelerators. Despite current market valuations suggesting a discount, AMD's operational signals indicate strong demand and growth in its strategic initiatives.

feed.title: DaVita Stock Sits at $151 With Analysts Pointing to a $190 High Target
feed.summary: DaVita (DVA) stock is currently priced at $150.7, with analysts setting a high target of $190. Recent positive developments include the first profitable year for its value-based care unit, strong Q4 earnings, and a significant beat in 2026 EPS guidance, driven by the resolution of a ransomware attack headwind and improved reimbursement rates. While Wall Street's consensus price target implies modest upside, TIKR's valuation model suggests a mid-case total return of 56.8% over 4.8 years, indicating that the market may be underpricing DaVita's growth potential.

feed.title: United States Antimony’s Growth Fuelled by $27M Defense Contract
feed.summary: United States Antimony Corporation (UAMY) has seen its stock rise by 12.8% following a $27 million contract from the Department of War to upgrade its Montana refinery and promising developments at its Fostung Tungsten deposit. The company is also forming a strategic partnership with Americas Gold and Silver to boost processing capacity and plans to uplist to the NYSE to increase market visibility. Despite some profitability challenges, UAMY aims to become a leading domestic antimony producer, leveraging these strategic moves and government contracts.

feed.title: Why Intel stock is surging over 4% on Monday
feed.summary: Intel's stock surged over 4% due to multiple positive developments, including new AI partnerships with Ericsson and Infosys and upcoming participation in Nvidia's GTC event. The company also showcased its new Core Series 2 processors and Health & Life Sciences AI Suite, contributing to renewed investor optimism. Unconfirmed reports of a potential $5 billion investment from Nvidia further fueled the stock's rise.

feed.title: Nvidia GTC 2026 Kicks Off in San Jose, Igniting a Tech-Wide Rally as 'Rubin' Architecture and Physical AI Take Center Stage
feed.summary: The Nvidia GTC 2026 conference in San Jose unveiled the new "Vera Rubin" architecture, boosting Nvidia shares and sparking a tech-wide rally focused on "Agentic" and "Physical" AI. This event signals a shift from generative AI to practical applications, with companies like Salesforce and Caterpillar showcasing advanced AI integrations. The conference also highlighted the critical need for energy efficiency, regulatory frameworks, and robust data foundations for the widespread adoption of autonomous AI agents.

feed.title: Why did Cingulate stock skyrocket today?
feed.summary: Cingulate Inc. (NASDAQ: CING) shares soared after the USPTO cleared a key patent application for its "Trimodal, Precision-Time Pulsatile Release Tablet," protecting its ADHD treatment CTx-1301. This regulatory win, combined with an upcoming PDUFA date on May 31 for CTx-1301, has significantly boosted investor confidence, with analysts predicting a further 150% upside. The drug's design aims to eliminate the need for afternoon booster doses for ADHD patients, targeting an $18 billion market.

feed.title: NVIDIA vs Microchip Technology: Which Is the Stronger Buy Today?
feed.summary: This article compares NVIDIA (NVDA) and Microchip Technology (MCHP) stocks, concluding that NVIDIA is the stronger buy. The analysis highlights NVDA's superior revenue growth, better profitability, and lower valuation compared to MCHP. It also presents detailed financial comparisons, historical market performance, and investment recommendations.

feed.title: How Weaker Earnings And Cautious 2026 Outlook At Li Auto (LI) Has Changed Its Investment Story
feed.summary: Li Auto Inc. recently reported disappointing Q4 2025 earnings and Q1 2026 guidance, indicating a decline in revenue and deliveries. The company is now relying on new flagship models, AI advancements, and overseas expansion in 2026 to counteract its weakened performance, while also addressing governance reforms. This shift challenges its previous optimistic growth forecast, urging investors to consider potential risks like price competition alongside these new growth strategies.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Ties Up With Adeia in a Multi-Year IP License Agreement
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ: AMD) has entered into a multi-year intellectual property license agreement with Adeia (ADEA), settling all previous legal disputes. This agreement grants AMD access to Adeia’s semiconductor IP portfolio and allows both companies to explore future collaboration in advanced semiconductor solutions. Separately, Truist Securities maintained its Buy rating for AMD with a $283 price target following AMD's partnership with Meta Platforms, where Meta will deploy AMD's Instinct GPUs and custom accelerators.

feed.title: At GTC, Cyngn Advances High-Fidelity Forklift Simulation Through FMU Integration in NVIDIA Isaac Sim
feed.summary: Cyngn (NASDAQ: CYN) announced the integration of its high-fidelity forklift vehicle models into NVIDIA Isaac Sim, an open simulation framework for autonomous systems development. This collaboration allows Cyngn to test forklift behavior in a virtual factory environment, reducing risk and accelerating deployment timelines for its autonomous forklift program, including a pre-order of 100 units by Arauco. The integration aligns with NVIDIA's vision for simulation as fundamental to physical autonomy across various industries.

feed.title: Intel Corp. Stock Consolidates at $45 as AI Gains Battle Foundry Losses
feed.summary: Intel Corp. stock is consolidating around $45, reflecting investor sentiment that balances strong AI infrastructure demand against persistent losses in its foundry segment. Analysts are largely rating the stock a "hold" as the company navigates strategic pivots and aims for foundry breakeven by 2027. While AI-driven PC and data center markets show promise, the execution risks and significant capital expenditures in the foundry business create uncertainty around near-term profitability.

feed.title: NXP Delivers New Innovations for Advanced Physical AI with NVIDIA
feed.summary: NXP Semiconductors announced new robotics solutions leveraging NVIDIA Holoscan Sensor Bridge to enable advanced physical AI applications, especially for humanoid robots. These solutions integrate NVIDIA technology with NXP's highly integrated SoCs to reduce development costs, shrink footprint, and simplify software complexity for secure, real-time data processing and transport. This collaboration aims to accelerate the development and deployment of physical AI by providing a unified architecture for complex tasks like sensor fusion, machine vision, and precision motor control.

feed.title: IBM-NVIDIA AI test cuts Nestlé data refresh to 3 minutes, saves 83% cost
feed.summary: IBM and NVIDIA have expanded their collaboration to enhance enterprise AI capabilities, focusing on GPU-native analytics, intelligent document processing, and hybrid cloud infrastructure. A key highlight is Nestlé's successful implementation, where a data refresh time was cut from 15 minutes to 3 minutes, resulting in an 83% cost saving and a 30X price-performance improvement. The partnership aims to help enterprises move from AI experimentation to full-scale production, with plans to offer NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra GPUs on IBM Cloud in early Q2 2026.

feed.title: Inside HPE’s €55M AI factories giving Europe and the US more control over data
feed.summary: HPE is establishing sovereign AI factories in Europe and the U.S. to enhance data control and foster AI innovation. Partnering with NVIDIA, HPE will build systems for the European Union's HammerHAI AI Factory in Germany and the Janus and Tara systems at Argonne National Laboratory in the U.S. These initiatives aim to provide secure, scalable, and compliant AI infrastructure for governments, research institutions, and businesses, allowing them to leverage AI while maintaining control over their data and intellectual property.

feed.title: Supermicro Reveals DCBBS® with New NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72, HGX Rubin NVL8, and Vera CPU Systems, Designed to Accelerate Customer Time-to-Market
feed.summary: Supermicro has unveiled its new system portfolio, including the NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72, HGX Rubin NVL8, and Vera CPU systems. These systems are designed with Supermicro's Data Center Building Block Solutions (DCBBS) advanced liquid-cooling technology stack to accelerate time-to-market for customers engaged in AI factory deployments. The company aims to provide high-performance, scalable solutions for agentic reasoning, long-context AI, and Mixture-of-Experts workloads, emphasizing efficiency and reduced total cost of ownership.

feed.title: Why Intel stock is surging over 4% on Monday
feed.summary: Intel's stock surged over 4% due to improved investor sentiment stemming from new AI and infrastructure partnerships, the broader semiconductor sector's rally before Nvidia GTC, and speculation about a $5 billion investment from Nvidia. The company also showcased new Core Series 2 processors and a Health & Life Sciences AI Suite, further boosting confidence in its turnaround strategy and manufacturing ambitions.

feed.title: Navitas Debuts Revolutionary 800 V-6 V Power Delivery Board at NVIDIA GTC 2026
feed.summary: Navitas Semiconductor has unveiled a new 800 V–6 V DC-DC power delivery board at NVIDIA GTC 2026, designed to eliminate the traditional 48 V intermediate bus converter stage in compute server trays. This innovation aims to enhance system efficiency, reliability, and compute density by enabling direct conversion from 800 V to 6 V. The solution targets high-power AI infrastructure, supporting the transition to 800 VDC data center power and improving overall system performance and power density.

feed.title: Supermicro unveils systems for NVIDIA Vera Rubin platform
feed.summary: Supermicro has announced a new portfolio of systems powered by the NVIDIA Vera Rubin platform, including the Vera Rubin NVL72, the 2U HGX Rubin NVL8, and Vera CPU systems, all utilizing Supermicro’s liquid-cooling technology. Despite challenging market conditions and a 31.5% share decline over the past six months, analysts consider the stock undervalued, with revenue growth of 35% in the last year. The company's recent strong Q2 2026 earnings and various new product launches underscore its continued innovation in the AI infrastructure space.

feed.title: Supermicro Reveals DCBBS® with New NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72, HGX Rubin NVL8, and Vera CPU Systems, Designed to Accelerate Customer Time-to-Market
feed.summary: Supermicro unveiled its new system portfolio featuring the NVIDIA Vera Rubin platform, including Vera Rubin NVL72, HGX Rubin NVL8, and Vera CPU systems. These are designed with Supermicro's Data Center Building Block Solutions (DCBBS) advanced liquid-cooling technology to accelerate time-to-market for customers. The new systems aim to meet the demands of AI factories, offering significant improvements in throughput, cost efficiency, and flexibility for AI training, inference, and high-performance computing workloads.

feed.title: Celestica-AMD Partner For AI Rack-Scale Platform Deal
feed.summary: Celestica Inc. is collaborating with Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. on its "Helios" rack-scale AI platform, focusing on research, development, design, and manufacturing of networking switches. This partnership aims to enhance AI infrastructure deployments across various sectors. While Celestica's stock shows strong growth and quality metrics, its low value rank suggests it may be trading at a premium.

feed.title: Adeia Inc stock hits 52-week high at 23.63 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Adeia Inc.'s stock reached a new 52-week high of $23.52, with current trading at $23.56, reflecting a 68.62% one-year total return. This growth is supported by a low PEG ratio (0.3) suggesting undervaluation and recent multi-year licensing deals with Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and United Microelectronics Corporation (UMC), which have led to increased price targets from analysts.

feed.title: Did Silicon Labs’ In‑Tire Sensor Deal With BANF Just Shift Silicon Laboratories’ (SLAB) Investment Narrative?
feed.summary: Silicon Labs recently collaborated with BANF to integrate its BG22 Bluetooth LE SoC into BANF's new in-tire, battery-free sensor platform for autonomous vehicles. This partnership allows tires to become high-value data sources, potentially shifting Silicon Labs' investment narrative by highlighting its ability to secure differentiated, security-focused designs in the automotive IoT sector. However, the article notes that this deal alone may not materially change near-term earnings risk, especially given the backdrop of Texas Instruments' potential acquisition of Silicon Labs.

feed.title: Onto Innovation Inc. $ONTO Stock Position Decreased by Aquatic Capital Management LLC
feed.summary: Aquatic Capital Management LLC significantly reduced its stake in Onto Innovation Inc. (NYSE:ONTO) by 76.7% in the third quarter, selling 110,539 shares and retaining 33,528 shares valued at $4.33 million. Despite this, other institutional investors like NewEdge Advisors LLC, Goldman Sachs Group Inc., UBS AM, Connor Clark & Lunn Investment Management Ltd., and Campbell & CO Investment Adviser LLC either increased their holdings or acquired new positions in ONTO. The company's stock performance shows a 52-week range of $85.88 to $232.49, and it currently holds a "Moderate Buy" rating with a consensus target price of $252.50 from analysts.

feed.title: Here’s Why Elite Hedge Funds Were Buying Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) For 2026
feed.summary: Elite hedge funds have been accumulating Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) shares amidst a 16% stake reduction by ARK and a year-to-date decline of 11%, seeing it as a buying opportunity due to significant AI deals with Meta, OpenAI, and Oracle. The company targets a 35% revenue CAGR through the decade and expects strong Q1 revenue growth, with its forward P/E down significantly from its peak. Analysts project AMD to reach $400+ by 2030 based on management's EPS guidance.

feed.title: Eco Wave Power Featured in NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang's GTC Keynote Video Highlighting the Role of Renewable Energy in the AI Era
feed.summary: Eco Wave Power's wave energy technology was highlighted in NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang's GTC keynote, showcasing its role in providing sustainable energy in the AI era. The presentation featured a digital twin of their technology, illustrating how AI-driven modeling can optimize renewable energy systems. This collaboration underscores the increasing importance of diversified renewable energy sources, particularly wave energy, to meet the growing electricity demands of AI and high-performance computing infrastructure.

feed.title: ‘This Changes the AI Cost Game,’ Says Morgan Stanley on Nvidia Stock (NVDA)
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley maintains its "Overweight" rating on Nvidia (NVDA), identifying it as a top pick due to its clear leadership in AI cost and performance. Following Nvidia's GTC 2026 keynote, analyst Joseph Moore reiterated the $260 price target, highlighting accelerating demand for AI infrastructure and Nvidia's growing advantage in cost per AI output. The firm believes the $1 trillion AI demand outlook for Blackwell and Vera Rubin systems could be conservative, with potential data center revenue reaching $1.1 trillion.

feed.title: Coherent Demonstrates Technologies for Next-Generation Pluggable Transceivers at OFC 2026
feed.summary: Coherent Corp. will showcase its comprehensive portfolio of next-generation pluggable optical technologies at OFC 2026, covering 1.6T, 3.2T, and emerging 12.8T architectures. The demonstrations will highlight Coherent's multi-technology platform strategy, including Silicon Photonics, Indium Phosphide, and VCSEL-based solutions for AI-driven data centers. The company aims to reinforce its role as an innovation partner for high-speed data center connectivity.

feed.title: Teradyne Introduces Photon 100
feed.summary: Teradyne, Inc. has launched the Photon 100, an automated opto-electric test platform designed for high-volume silicon photonics and co-packaged optics manufacturing. This new platform integrates optical and electrical instrumentation with Teradyne’s UltraFLEXplus platform to accelerate production, reduce complexity, and meet the growing demands of AI and next-generation data centers. The Photon 100 aims to simplify operations and speed up time-to-market for manufacturers in the rapidly expanding SiPh and CPO industries.

feed.title: Silicon Motion Technology and Eos Energy Enterprises have been highlighted as Zacks Bull and Bear of the Day
feed.summary: Zacks Equity Research has named Silicon Motion Technology (SIMO) as its Bull of the Day due to its strong performance in NAND flash storage devices, benefiting from AI buildout demand, and Eos Energy Enterprises (EOSE) as its Bear of the Day following a significant earnings miss and subsequent stock drop. The article also provides a detailed analysis of Micron Technology (MU) ahead of its Q2 earnings report, highlighting strong demand for HBM and NAND chips due to AI infrastructure growth, making it a "Strong Buy" according to Zacks. Nvidia (NVDA) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) are also mentioned in the context of Micron's HBM supply for GPU competitors.

feed.title: Intel Corporation Wins Spot in Nvidia’s Rubin AI Servers With Xeon 6
feed.summary: Intel's Xeon 6 chips have secured a spot as host CPUs in Nvidia’s DGX Rubin NVL8 AI servers, highlighting the increasing importance of CPUs as AI spending shifts to inference. While this is a significant design win for Intel, the company still faces challenges with its broader turnaround, including regaining manufacturing clout with its 18A process and addressing soft yields and fluctuating market demand. Despite these hurdles, Intel also recently launched new Core Ultra 200HX Plus mobile chips, targeting gaming and workstation laptops.

feed.title: F5 (FFIV) Enhances AI Inference with Nvidia Partnership
feed.summary: F5 (FFIV) has expanded its partnership with Nvidia to enhance AI inference infrastructure by integrating F5's BIG-IP Next for Kubernetes with Nvidia's BlueField-3 DPUs, aiming to increase token throughput, lower latency, and secure multi-tenant AI platforms. The company, a leader in application delivery, shows strong financial health with robust margins and a sound balance sheet, though insider selling warrants investor attention.

feed.title: Celestica vs. Arista: Which AI Infrastructure Stock is a Better Buy?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Celestica (CLS) and Arista Networks (ANET) as key players in the rapidly growing AI infrastructure market, which is expected to reach $72.02 billion by 2025. It compares their individual strengths, market positions, challenges, and financial outlook based on Zacks estimates and price performance. Concluding that Celestica is a better investment due to its superior Zacks Rank, better price performance, and more attractive valuation despite facing similar market opportunities.

feed.title: STMicro Partners with Leopard Imaging on a Three-In-One Smart Vision Platform for NVIDIA's Jetson
feed.summary: STMicroelectronics has partnered with Leopard Imaging to create a "multimodal vision module" compatible with NVIDIA's Jetson platform and Holoscan Sensor Bridge. This innovative module integrates imaging, 3D scene mapping, and motion detection, aiming to accelerate the development of physical AI applications and humanoid robotics. It features three key STMicro sensors: a 5.1-megapixel image sensor, a direct time-of-flight lidar module for 3D depth sensing, and a six-axis inertial measurement unit with an on-board machine learning core.

feed.title: GDS Holdings Ltd’s 2025Q4 Earnings Call: Chip Supply Certainty Clashes With Project Timelines, Market Supply Dynamics at Odds With Pricing Trends
feed.summary: GDS Holdings Ltd reported strong financial results for FY 2025, with an 11% increase in revenue and adjusted EBITDA, driven by a recovery in data center demand and significant AI-related bookings. The company plans to expand into new growth markets and aims for substantial new bookings in 2026, leveraging improved chip supply and strategic asset monetization. While chip supply is more certain, long project delivery timelines and potential MSR declines temper overall growth projections, although strong demand is expected to offset these factors.

feed.title: Onto Innovation Inc. (NYSE:ONTO) Sees Significant Increase in Short Interest
feed.summary: Onto Innovation Inc. (NYSE:ONTO) experienced a significant increase in short interest in February, rising 16.1% to 1,165,350 shares, representing 2.4% of outstanding shares and a 0.9 days-to-cover ratio. Despite this, several analysts have raised their price targets, with Cantor Fitzgerald upgrading the stock to "overweight" and the consensus rating remaining a "Moderate Buy" with a $252.50 target. The company reported Q4 EPS of $1.26, slightly missing estimates, and set Q1 2026 EPS guidance between $1.26 and $1.36.

feed.title: Supermicro Reveals DCBBS with New NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72, HGX Rubin NVL8, and Vera CPU Systems
feed.summary: Supermicro has unveiled its upcoming system portfolio featuring the NVIDIA Vera Rubin platform, designed to meet the escalating demands of large-scale AI workloads. These new systems, including the NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72, NVIDIA HGX Rubin NVL8, and NVIDIA Vera CPU systems, integrate Supermicro’s Data Center Building Block Solutions (DCBBS) with advanced liquid-cooling technology for rapid deployment. The initiative aims to provide customers with infrastructure capable of powering next-generation AI factories efficiently, supporting agentic reasoning, long-context AI, and Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) workloads.

feed.title: Infleqtion deploys 100-physical-qubit quantum computer at UK’s NQCC
feed.summary: Infleqtion has deployed the UK's first operational 100-physical-qubit quantum computer at the National Quantum Computing Centre (NQCC), marking a significant advancement in the UK's quantum capabilities. This deployment uses Infleqtion's Sqale platform, a neutral-atom system designed to facilitate research and development in large-scale quantum computing. Concurrently, the UK government announced a £1 billion quantum funding pledge to retain homegrown talent and advance its quantum technology leadership.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. stock faces pressure despite Q4 AI momentum and 12GW hyperscaler deals
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) reported record Q4 2025 data center revenue of $5.4 billion, up 39% year-over-year, with significant AI infrastructure deals including 12 gigawatts of GPU deployments for Meta and OpenAI. Despite this strong performance and upcoming MI450 GPU and Venice CPU launches in H2 2026, the stock has faced pressure in early 2026, trading 28% below its 52-week high due to competitive concerns and high market expectations. Analysts, particularly in the German-speaking market, are closely watching the MI450 ramp-up for potential upside, balancing the promising growth trajectory against execution risks and competition from Nvidia.

feed.title: The Bull Case For Texas Instruments (TXN) Could Change Following Its New 800V AI Power Architecture
feed.summary: Texas Instruments (TXN) unveiled a new 800V direct current power architecture for AI data centers, developed in collaboration with NVIDIA. This innovation aims to streamline power conversion and deepen TI's integration into AI infrastructure, potentially reshaping its investment narrative. While this move and other AI-related product launches offer growth opportunities, investors are cautioned about possible risks from high capital expenditures and potential market oversupply.

feed.title: Invesco Summit Fund Q4 2025 Portfolio Positioning
feed.summary: The Invesco Summit Fund significantly overweighted industrials, energy, and communication services in Q4 2025, driven by anticipated benefits from AI-related energy demand and increased global defense spending. The fund added AMD and Fabrinet due to their strong positions in AI and data center growth, while exiting KKR, Blackstone, and Oracle over technical struggles and high debt, reallocating capital to AI-focused holdings. Financials were held at close to equal weight, expected to benefit from tax cuts and bank deregulation.

feed.summary: This article provides price, market cap, charts, and fundamental information for the iShares Semiconductor ETF (Ondo Tokenized) (SOXXON). feed.title: Inside the 25dBm laser behind next-gen AI data centers at OFC 2026
feed.summary: Applied Optoelectronics (NASDAQ: AAOI) will showcase its 25dBm ultra-high power ELSFP and a 6.4T On-Board Optics (OBO) demo at OFC 2026, positioning these as foundational for next-gen AI data center infrastructure. The company also announced plans for a 210,000 sq ft U.S. manufacturing facility near Sugar Land, Texas, to scale production of AI-focused transceivers. An investor session will be held on March 17 at 4:00 p.m. PT.

feed.title: Assessing SkyWater Technology (SKYT) Valuation After Its Strong 1 Year Total Return
feed.summary: SkyWater Technology (SKYT) has seen its stock rise over 200% in the past year, sparking investor interest in its current valuation. Despite recent short-term cooling, the company's expansion into quantum computing and advanced packaging supports a fair value narrative of $35.00, suggesting it may be undervalued compared to its closing price of $27.86. However, investors are advised to consider integration risks and exposure to government contracts.

feed.title: A Look At Tower Semiconductor (NasdaqGS:TSEM) Valuation After New AI Photonics And Power Management Announcements
feed.summary: Tower Semiconductor (TSEM) has seen a significant increase in its stock price following new AI-related announcements, specifically a Gen3 BCD power management platform and a silicon photonics networking collaboration. While a valuation model suggests the company is undervalued at $159.29 compared to its current share price of $138.47, driven by its RF and silicon photonics positioning, concerns remain regarding execution risks and a high P/E ratio compared to the industry average. Investors are advised to conduct their own due diligence given the divided opinions and strong sentiment.

feed.title: F5 and NVIDIA Advance AI Factory Economics With New Capabilities for Accelerated AI Inference
feed.summary: F5 and NVIDIA are collaborating to enhance AI inference infrastructure by integrating F5 BIG-IP Next for Kubernetes with NVIDIA BlueField-3 DPUs. This partnership aims to improve "tokenomics" — increasing token throughput, reducing latency, and enabling secure multi-tenant AI platforms at scale. The joint solution has shown significant performance gains, such as a 40% increase in token throughput and 61% faster time to first token, by offloading networking and security functions to DPUs, thus maximizing GPU utilization and economic output per accelerator.

feed.title: AMD and NAVER Cloud Announce Strategic Collaboration to Advance AI Infrastructure in Korea
feed.summary: AMD and NAVER Cloud have announced a strategic collaboration to accelerate the development of sovereign AI infrastructure in Korea. This partnership will involve NAVER Cloud expanding its use of AMD EPYC processors and gaining early access to AMD Instinct MI455X GPUs for evaluation. The companies will also work together to optimize NAVER Cloud's AI services and software stacks on AMD platforms.

feed.title: Alibaba Hikes AI Computing Prices Up to 34% After Demand Soars
feed.summary: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. has increased prices for its AI computing and storage products by up to 34% to capitalize on the soaring demand for AI services. This move includes a 5% to 34% hike for its T-Head AI computing chips and a 30% increase for its Cloud Parallel File Storage service. The price adjustments reflect the company's strategy to recoup substantial investments in AI technology.

feed.title: Is Aehr Test Systems’ (AEHR) New AI Test Order Redefining Its Core Competitive Position?
feed.summary: Aehr Test Systems recently secured a US$14,000,000 order for its FOX-XP wafer-level test systems from a prominent AI processor customer, reinforcing its position in semiconductor test equipment for AI and data center applications. This order strengthens Aehr's AI test thesis and contributes to its second-half fiscal 2026 revenue guidance, despite the company's current unprofitability and high valuation. Investors should consider the execution risk and high reliance on a few key customers when evaluating AEHR's potential.

feed.title: K-Tech Partners Boardware On AI, BCI Hardware
feed.summary: K-Tech Solutions Company Limited has partnered with Boardware Intelligence Technology Limited as the exclusive hardware development partner for Boardware's AI and Brain-Computer Interface (BCI) product platform. K-Tech will apply its engineering expertise to commercialize Boardware's "Barco" ecosystem, which includes Barco AR Glasses, BarcoMind, and BarcoEar. This collaboration aims to accelerate the development of AI-enabled hardware, with market entry anticipated in late 2026.

feed.title: AMD CEO deepens ties with Samsung, Naver in AI infrastructure push
feed.summary: AMD CEO Lisa Su visited South Korea to strengthen collaborations with Samsung Electronics and Naver on AI infrastructure. Samsung and AMD signed an MoU to expand their strategic partnership on next-generation AI memory and computing technologies, including HBM4 and advanced DRAM solutions. Su also met with Naver CEO Choi Soo-yeon to develop a more integrated AI ecosystem and enhance technical collaboration for Naver's hyperscale AI model, HyperClova X.

feed.title: Samsung and AMD Expand Strategic Collaboration on Next-Generation AI Memory Solutions
feed.summary: Samsung Electronics and AMD have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to expand their strategic collaboration on next-generation AI memory and computing technologies. This partnership will focus on industry-leading HBM4 supply for AMD Instinct MI455X GPUs and advanced DDR5 solutions for AMD EPYC processors and the AMD Helios platform. The companies aim to deliver optimized AI infrastructure for customers, with potential future discussions around foundry partnership.

feed.title: Korea, Taiwan chip sectors most exposed to helium shortage amid Middle East war
feed.summary: A Fitch Ratings report indicates that South Korea and Taiwan's semiconductor sectors are highly vulnerable to helium shortages due to the Middle East conflict, as both rely heavily on Qatar for their helium imports. Japan, in contrast, has a more diversified supply chain. The ongoing conflict has disrupted Qatar's LNG production and constrained shipments through the Strait of Hormuz, posing medium-term risks if disruptions persist.

feed.title: Why Magnachip (MX) Stock Is Up Today
feed.summary: Magnachip Semiconductor (MX) stock rose by 2.4% following insider purchases by an affiliated entity of director Nathan Gilbert E, signaling confidence in the company's future. The purchases, totaling 38,006 shares, increased the entity's holdings to 136,206 shares. This move also aligns with positive sentiment in the semiconductor industry driven by AI and high-performance computing growth.

feed.title: Danica Pension Takes $11.64 Million Position in Applied Materials
feed.summary: Danica Pension Livsforsikringsaktieselskab, a Danish pension fund, acquired a new position in Applied Materials, Inc. (NASDAQ:AMAT) during the third quarter, purchasing 56,830 shares valued at approximately $11.64 million. This investment signals the fund's confidence in the semiconductor equipment manufacturer's long-term growth prospects, as the company's performance is closely tied to the global electronics industry.

feed.title: Samsung agrees to supply HBM4 to AMD; wider AI chip foundry alliance under discussion
feed.summary: Samsung Electronics has agreed to supply its latest HBM4 memory chips to AMD, signifying intensified collaboration in the AI chip sector. AMD CEO Lisa Su met with Samsung Chairman Jay Y. Lee and other Korean tech executives during her visit to Seoul. This partnership comes amid broader discussions about an AI chip foundry alliance, illustrating the growing strategic importance of HBM technology and advanced chip manufacturing.

feed.title: Why Magnachip (MX) Stock Is Up Today
feed.summary: Magnachip Semiconductor (MX) stock rose 3% today after insider purchases were reported by a director's affiliated entity, GT Investments II Corp. The entity bought 38,006 shares between March 12-13, increasing its holdings and signaling strong confidence in the company. The semiconductor industry's positive sentiment due to AI and high-performance computing also contributed to the rise.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD) Eyes Samsung Collaboration on AI Chipsets
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) is reportedly seeking a collaboration with Samsung Electronics to discuss the supply of high-bandwidth memory (HBM) for artificial intelligence chipsets. AMD CEO Lisa Su is expected to meet with Samsung Chairman Jay Y. Lee in South Korea to explore cooperation in building out data centers and AI systems, as well as computing technologies. This potential partnership highlights AMD's focus on the data center and AI markets, reinforcing its role as an infrastructure provider for major tech companies in the AI and metaverse space.

feed.title: AMD’s Lisa Su Signals Potential Chip Supply for Naver’s AI Data Centers
feed.summary: AMD CEO Lisa Su met with Naver CEO Choi Soo-yeon to discuss potentially supplying AMD's AI-focused chips for Naver's expanding "Gak Sejong" AI data centers. Naver is looking to diversify its chip supply beyond Nvidia and is considering GPUs and CPUs from AMD and Intel to build customized racks and reduce costs. The discussions included AMD’s Instinct GPUs and EPYC CPUs, and Su is also exploring collaborations with Samsung Electronics’ foundry business.
feed.source: THE ELEC, Korea Electronics Industry Media
feed.source_domain: THE ELEC, Korea Electronics Industry Media

feed.title: Nvidia: Raising Fair Value as ‘Agentic AI’ Drives a $1 Trillion Forecast at GTC
feed.summary: Morningstar has raised its fair value estimate for Nvidia stock to $260 from $240, citing an "agentic AI" driven $1 trillion forecast for Blackwell and Rubin AI products from 2025-27. Despite the market's flattish reaction, Morningstar believes Nvidia is materially undervalued, as the AI ecosystem remains computing-constrained. The forecast implies robust growth for Nvidia's data center revenue, supplemented by new low-latency processors and support for open-source "agentic" AI.

feed.title: Captrust Financial Advisors Sells 172,149 Shares of Palo Alto Networks
feed.summary: Captrust Financial Advisors has reduced its stake in Palo Alto Networks Inc. by selling 172,149 shares in the third quarter. This sale decreased their holdings by 19.7%, leaving them with 702,861 shares valued at approximately $143.1 million. The move suggests a potential shift in the institutional investor's outlook on the cybersecurity firm.

feed.title: Dorsey Wright & Associates Invests $6.56 Million in Comfort Systems USA, Inc. $FIX
feed.summary: Dorsey Wright & Associates initiated a new position in Comfort Systems USA (FIX), purchasing 7,946 shares valued at $6.56 million, making it their 21st largest holding. Despite this institutional investment, company insiders have been net sellers, disposing of over $53 million in shares in the last three months. Comfort Systems USA recently reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst expectations, and increased its quarterly dividend.

feed.title: Chiplet innovation isn’t waiting for perfect standards
feed.summary: The article discusses how chiplet technology is being adopted rapidly across various industries like AI, HPC, and automotive, even though universal standards are still in development. It highlights the advantages of chiplets over monolithic SoCs, such as better yields, cost reduction, and the ability to combine diverse process technologies. The author emphasizes that companies are progressing with chiplet innovation by using modular designs and automated integration, rather than waiting for fully mature standards.

feed.title: 11 Most Buzzing Stocks to Buy with the Highest Upside Potential
feed.summary: NeoGenomics Inc. (NASDAQ: NEO) is highlighted as one of the most buzzing stocks with high upside potential, following the launch of its RaDaR ST assay. This new diagnostic tool targets the rapidly growing molecular residual disease (MRD) market, estimated at over $20 billion, by offering early detection of residual disease and recurrence in cancer patients. The advanced assay uses whole-exome sequencing and has received Medicare approval, significantly expanding NeoGenomics' oncology portfolio.

feed.title: Why Is AMD (Advanced Micro Devices) Stock Rising In Premarket Today?
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) shares are up over 1% in premarket trading after announcing a major memory supply agreement with Samsung. Samsung will be the primary supplier of HBM4 memory for AMD's next-generation MI455X AI accelerators and will also provide DDR5 memory chips. This news contributed to a broader premarket rally in chip stocks, though retail sentiment on Stocktwits for AMD remains largely bearish.

feed.title: Analysts Offer Insights on Technology Companies: Navan, Inc. Class A (NAVN), Bandwidth (BAND) and Nvidia (NVDA)
feed.summary: This article summarizes bullish analyst ratings for three technology companies: Navan, Inc. Class A (NAVN), Bandwidth (BAND), and Nvidia (NVDA). Citi analyst Steve Enders reiterated a Buy rating for Navan with a $26.00 price target, while Needham's Joshua Reilly maintained a Buy rating for Bandwidth with a $20.00 price target. KeyBanc's John Vinh also maintained a Buy rating for Nvidia, setting a price target of $275.00.

feed.title: Can Celestica's Tie-Up With AMD for AI Switches Stoke Growth?
feed.summary: Celestica Inc. has partnered with Advanced Micro Devices Inc. to develop and produce scale-up networking switches for AMD's new Helios AI platform, aiming to accelerate large-scale AI deployments. This collaboration leverages Celestica's expertise to provide high-speed interconnectivity for AMD's next-generation Instinct MI450 Series GPUs, strengthening Celestica's position in the AI networking solutions market. Despite competition from companies like Arista Networks and NVIDIA, Celestica shows strong financial performance and positive earnings estimates, indicating potential for continued growth.

feed.title: IShares Trust - iShares Semiconductor ETF announces Quarterly dividend, payable on March 20, 2026

The ETF aims to track the performance of US-listed semiconductor stocks as represented by the PHLX Semiconductor Sector Index.

feed.title: Celestica: Time To Bet Bigger (NYSE:CLS)
feed.summary: Celestica (CLS) has been upgraded from 'Buy' to 'Strong Buy' due to improving fundamentals, attractive valuation after a recent price dip, and its strategic positioning in the AI infrastructure market. The company is benefiting from hyperscaler AI infrastructure investments and expanding its market reach through a partnership with AMD on the Helios rack-scale AI platform. Despite risks like rising customer concentration (80% of sales from top 10 customers) and execution challenges from a large $1 billion CapEx cycle, CLS's forward P/E for FY2028 and non-GAAP PEG ratio indicate a compelling valuation.

feed.title: Supermicro Advances Enterprises' Adoption of Accelerated Computing Across AI Factory, Data Center, and Edge with Expanded Portfolio Featuring NVIDIA RTX PRO Blackwell Server Edition GPUs
feed.summary: Supermicro is expanding its portfolio of enterprise solutions to bolster the adoption of accelerated computing across AI Factory, data centers, and edge environments. The new offerings feature NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs and NVIDIA Vera CPUs, enabling enhanced acceleration and efficiency for diverse workloads. Supermicro's NVIDIA-Certified Systems ensure compatibility and accelerate enterprise applications, providing flexible, modular solutions for varied infrastructure needs.

feed.title: Supermicro Advances Enterprises' Adoption of Accelerated Computing Across AI Factory, Data Center, and Edge with Expanded Portfolio Featuring NVIDIA RTX PRO Blackwell Server Edition GPUs
feed.summary: Supermicro is expanding its portfolio with new systems featuring NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs and NVIDIA Vera CPUs, designed for enterprise AI and graphical computing applications. These solutions aim to provide high-density compute infrastructure in space, power, and thermally-restricted environments across data centers, cloud, and edge deployments. Supermicro's NVIDIA-Certified Systems offer validated compatibility and support for a wide range of accelerated enterprise workloads, aiding in faster AI adoption and infrastructure ROI.

feed.title: Roundhill AMD WeeklyPay ETF declares weekly distribution of $0.6410
feed.summary: The Roundhill AMD WeeklyPay ETF (NYSE Arca: AMDW) has declared a weekly distribution of $0.6410 per share for its shareholders. This distribution reflects the ETF's strategy of generating income from its holdings.

feed.title: AMD-Samsung Expand Power Alliance To Resolve AI Data Bottlenecks
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ: AMD) and Samsung Electronics Co (OTC: SSNLF) are strengthening their alliance to develop next-generation AI memory solutions, focusing on addressing data bottlenecks in the rapidly expanding AI infrastructure. This collaboration emphasizes memory performance and power efficiency for AI compute platforms, directly supporting AMD's data center and AI roadmap. The partnership aims to efficiently feed GPUs and accelerators as AI model sizes continue to grow, highlighting the critical role of advanced memory in scaling AI capabilities.

feed.title: Q/C Technologies Launches Optical Processing Unit (OPU) Initiative to Develop Proprietary Silicon Photonic Computing Architecture for AI Inference
feed.summary: Q/C Technologies, Inc. has announced the launch of its Optical Processing Unit (OPU) initiative to develop a proprietary silicon photonic computing architecture for AI inference. This initiative aims to address the performance and energy limitations of traditional electronic architectures, particularly GPUs, in handling complex AI operations like matrix multiplication. The company plans to overcome existing challenges in optical computing through patentable technology and strategic partnerships, with the goal of creating an optical chip that enhances bandwidth, energy efficiency, and scalability for AI hardware.

feed.title: Is Aehr Test Systems’ (AEHR) New AI Test Order Redefining Its Core Competitive Position?
feed.summary: Aehr Test Systems recently received a US$14 million order for its FOX-XP wafer-level test systems from a prominent AI processor customer, signifying increased demand for its AI and data center testing technology. This order emphasizes Aehr's role as a specialist in semiconductor test equipment for AI and data center components, though the company currently remains unprofitable and heavily reliant on a few customers. The article suggests that while the large AI-driven order supports Aehr's investment narrative, investors should be aware of execution risks and its rich valuation.

feed.title: Can Celestica's Tie-Up With AMD for AI Switches Stoke Growth?
feed.summary: Celestica Inc. has partnered with Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. to power the new Helios rack-scale AI platform, with Celestica leading the R&D, design, and production of scale-up networking switches for AMD's next-generation Instinct MI450 Series GPUs. This collaboration bolsters Celestica's position in the advanced data center and AI networking solutions market. Despite competition from companies like Arista Networks and NVIDIA, Celestica's shares have significantly outperformed the industry, and its earnings estimates for 2026 and 2027 show strong growth.

feed.title: Celestica (TSX:CLS) Stock Price
feed.summary: Celestica Inc. (TSX:CLS) is a supply chain solutions provider experiencing rapid growth due to strong demand in advanced networking and AI for hyperscalers. Analysts project significant future revenue growth, highlighting strategic diversification and a focus on high-margin markets.

feed.title: Supermicro unveils AI systems based on NVIDIA Vera Rubin
feed.summary: Supermicro has unveiled its new portfolio of AI systems, powered by the NVIDIA Vera Rubin platform, designed to meet the growing demand for AI factory infrastructure. These systems include the NVL72, HGX NVL8, and Vera CPU systems, all featuring Supermicro’s Data Center Building Block Solutions (DCBBS) with advanced liquid-cooling technology for rapid deployment and reduced TCO. The Vera Rubin NVL72, for instance, operates as a rack-scale AI supercomputer, capable of delivering up to 3.6 exaflops of inference, making it suitable for large-scale AI training, inference, and accelerated HPC workloads.

feed.title: Smart money is betting big on Datavault stock ahead of earnings
feed.summary: Institutional investors like Vanguard, State Street, and BlackRock have significantly increased their stakes in Datavault AI Inc. (NASDAQ: DVLT) ahead of its Q4 earnings report. The AI company's stock has already risen nearly 50% recently, and analysts at Maxim Group have reiterated a "buy" rating with a price target suggesting it could more than quadruple. Investors are looking for sustainable high-margin scalability and updates on the Josh Gibson Coin dividend in the upcoming earnings.

feed.title: The Nvidia GTC Has Been Very Bullish For AMD (NASDAQ:AMD)
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is positioned to outperform Nvidia in the evolving AI infrastructure market. AMD's open-source Helios architecture and superior memory capacity, particularly with the MI455X GPU, are better suited to address critical AI bottlenecks than Nvidia's offerings. Hyperscalers' desire to avoid vendor lock-in, coupled with AMD's memory and CPU advantages, are expected to help AMD close the valuation gap with Nvidia in 2026.

feed.title: Assessing Texas Instruments (TXN) Valuation After New 800V AI Data Center Power Architecture Announcement
feed.summary: Texas Instruments (TXN) has announced a new 800V DC power architecture for AI data centers, built around NVIDIA’s reference design, which is expected to reset expectations around its role in data center and edge computing. Despite a recent pullback, TXN shares are up year-to-date and over one year. Valuation narratives present mixed views, with some analysts seeing the stock as undervalued based on future capacity expansion and margin improvements, while a DCF model suggests it is expensive.

feed.title: DigitalOcean jumps 7.7% as AI-infrastructure narrative stays hot after upbeat outlook and new GPU product updates
feed.summary: DigitalOcean (DOCN) stock rose 7.7% due to continued momentum from its AI-focused strategy, positive quarterly results, and stronger forward revenue outlook for 2026 and 2027. The company recently expanded its GPU lineup for its AI cloud with new AMD-powered GPU Droplets, reinforcing demand for GPU-based inference workloads. This rally suggests investors are repricing the stock based on improved growth visibility and successful adoption of its AI offerings.

feed.title: Cisco is Overvalued at 4.95X PS: Should You Still Buy the Stock?
feed.summary: Cisco Systems (CSCO) shares are considered overvalued based on a 4.95X forward 12-month price/sales ratio, exceeding the industry average. Despite this, the company's strong prospects are driven by its AI infrastructure push, including an expected $3 billion in AI revenues from hyperscalers by fiscal 2026, and an expanding networking portfolio with robust growth in product orders. Cisco recently provided positive guidance for Q3 and fiscal year 2026, and the stock currently holds a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy).

feed.title: NXP Semiconductors (NasdaqGS:NXPI) Stock Price
feed.summary: This article provides an overview of NXP Semiconductors' stock performance, analyst sentiments, and key financial details. The company's financials, recent dividends, and main competitors are also presented.

feed.title: DigitalOcean Boosts Performance as AI Lab Migrates Workloads to Cloud
feed.summary: DigitalOcean's stock has risen over 10% following strong Q4 financial results, including a revenue increase of 18% and annualized run-rate revenue surpassing $1 billion. These developments position DigitalOcean as a key player in providing cost-efficient solutions for AI development and expanding its digital footprint.

feed.title: Intel Jumps 2.8% in Afternoon Trading—What’s Driving the Uptrend?

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) and Celestica Bring Open-Standard ‘Helios’ AI Platform to Market

feed.title: New quantum method targets a battery problem classical computing can't solve
feed.summary: Xanadu, in collaboration with the University of Toronto and the National Research Council of Canada, has unveiled a novel quantum algorithm designed to accelerate simulations of lithium-excess cathode materials for next-generation batteries. This method allows for Resonant Inelastic X-ray Scattering (RIXS) simulations beyond classical computing capabilities with fewer than 500 logical qubits, making it feasible for early utility-scale fault-tolerant quantum computers. Despite this scientific breakthrough, the market reacted negatively, with Xanadu's stock (CHAC) declining 9.30%.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) and Celestica Bring Open-Standard ‘Helios’ AI Platform to Market

feed.title: New photon-counting CT aims to spot tiny lesions in a single scan

This new system, powered by Deep Silicon detector technology, offers ultra-high definition spatial and spectral imaging to visualize subtle tissue variations and small lesions with high precision in a single scan. The technology is designed to improve diagnostic accuracy, reduce complexity for clinicians, and is expected to drive 1-2% revenue growth for GE HealthCare.

feed.title: Power Integrations Extends Flyback Topology to Enable 440 W, Offering Simpler Alternatives to Resonant Power Designs

The new ICs provide 92% efficiency and meet stringent energy regulations for standby power consumption, making them suitable for high-end appliances, e-bike chargers, and industrial applications.

The stock had previously dropped due to news of federal export control violations involving another company, Super Micro Computer, but with this new announcement, investor confidence in the chip industry has been boosted.

feed.title: InterDigital Report Examines Network Impact of Agentic AI Growth

Savvy investors are advised to monitor insider activity and the underlying stock's ability to sustain its rally, as backtesting shows a mean reversion strategy on IREN yielded negative returns.

feed.title: United Microelectronics stock gains on NYSE amid AI-driven semiconductor recovery and annual meeting

The company benefits from surging AI demand, particularly through its focus on mature nodes and power management technologies, providing a value investment opportunity for US investors in the AI chip supply chain without the premium of advanced node leaders like TSMC. Analysts note UMC's steady sales momentum and improving fab utilization rates, making it a resilient pick for diversified portfolios.

feed.title: United Microelectronics stock gains on NYSE amid AI-driven semiconductor recovery and annual meeting
feed.summary: United Microelectronics (UMC) stock rose 2.87% on the NYSE to $9.31, driven by the broader semiconductor sector's momentum from AI demand and the announcement of its 2026 Annual Shareholders' Meeting. UMC offers US investors a value play in foundry services, focusing on mature nodes and power-efficient processes for various electronics, positioning it as a resilient pick for diversified portfolios. The company is benefiting from surging AI infrastructure needs, with increased fab utilization and strategic focus on specialty processes like 55nm BCD, appealing to hyperscalers and edge AI growth.

feed.title: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Ltd. $TSM Shares Sold by HWG Holdings LP
feed.summary: HWG Holdings LP significantly reduced its stake in Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM) by 94% in the fourth quarter, selling 28,655 shares and retaining 1,819 shares worth approximately $553,000. Despite this sell-off, TSM is experiencing positive catalysts including high demand for its advanced nodes, a large order from ASML, and insider buying, indicating sustained growth. The company maintains a strong market cap of $1.80 trillion, a net margin of 45.13%, and announced a quarterly dividend of $0.9503.

feed.title: KLA Corporation stock dips 1.44% on Nasdaq amid semiconductor inspection demand pressures and AI cycle shifts
feed.summary: KLA Corporation's stock dropped 1.44% on Nasdaq to $1,543.68 due to investor concerns over softening demand for wafer inspection tools in the semiconductor industry, stemming from post-AI boom inventory adjustments. Despite a strong year-to-date performance, the dip reflects tactical profit-taking and fears of normalizing demand from hyperscalers. Analysts note KLA's critical role in AI chip yield management, but highlight risks from capex uncertainty and cycle timing.

feed.title: Can AI Data Center Demand Accelerate ADI's Long-Term Growth?
feed.summary: Analog Devices (ADI) is experiencing significant growth in its data center business, driven by increasing AI demand for power and optical solutions. The company's products for power management and optical connectivity are crucial for AI servers, which require more power and intricate control. While facing competition from Broadcom and Advanced Micro Devices, ADI's performance and valuation suggest a strong potential for long-term growth, with analysts forecasting substantial revenue and earnings increases for fiscal years 2026 and 2027.

feed.title: Why Is Advanced Micro Devices Stock Trading Lower On Thursday?
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock is trading lower on Thursday after a previous rally, amidst reports that both AMD and Intel plan to increase CPU prices by 10-15% due to semiconductor supply shortages. The strained supply is exacerbated by rising AI demand, which prioritizes large customers, leaving PC makers facing wider supply gaps. Geopolitical risks and material shortages also contribute to volatility in the broader semiconductor sector.

feed.title: OFC 2026: Marvell Launches Industry’s First 260-lane PCIe 6.0 Switch for AI Data Center Scale-up Infrastructure
feed.summary: Marvell Technology, Inc. has launched the Structera S 60260, the industry's first 260-lane PCIe 6.0 switch, significantly enhancing AI data center scale-up infrastructure. This new switch, incorporating technology from the acquisition of XConn Technologies, offers double the lane density of competitive products, reducing power consumption, latency, and costs in AI server environments. It expands Marvell's end-to-end PCIe portfolio, providing flexible and high-performance interconnect solutions for demanding AI and machine learning workloads.
