feed.title: Intel Corp Stock (INTC) Moved Down by 4.02% on Mar 26: What Signal Does It Send?
feed.summary: Intel Corp (INTC) stock declined by 4.02% on March 26th, influenced by broader market weakness and profit-taking despite recent positive news like CPU price increases and new chip launches. Analysts largely maintain a "Reduce" rating due to persistent concerns about Intel's foundry business issues, cautious financial guidance, and intense competition in the AI and server chip markets. Technical indicators show a neutral to oversold condition, while media coverage remains low.

feed.title: Eyelit Technologies Recognized in the 2026 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Supply Chain Planning Solutions: Discrete Industries
feed.summary: Eyelit Technologies has been recognized as a Niche Player in the 2026 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Supply Chain Planning Solutions: Discrete Industries. The recognition highlights Eyelit's strengths in automating manufacturing decisions, integrating planning with real-time execution data, and its unique Attribute-Based Planning approach. CEO Joe Bellini emphasizes that this validates their ability to deliver intelligent planning solutions deeply connected to manufacturing execution, leading to improved throughput and resource utilization for discrete manufacturers.

feed.title: [News] STMicroelectronics Reportedly to Raise Prices from Apr. 26; Intel, AMD Said to Plan CPU Hikes of 10–15%
feed.summary: STMicroelectronics is reportedly planning to increase prices across several product lines starting April 26, 2026, due to rising material costs, energy expenses, and capacity constraints. This move reflects a broader trend in the semiconductor industry, with companies like onsemi, NXP, Texas Instruments, and Infineon also adjusting prices. Intel and AMD are also said to be planning CPU price hikes of 10–15% due to sustained demand, increased costs, and tightening supply, leading to longer lead times for CPUs.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Stock Rises 7% on CPU Price Hike Reports
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock rose 6.6% on March 25, 2026, driven by reports of impending CPU price hikes of 10-15% and potential deals to supply AI accelerators to a South Korean startup. The price increases, implemented across AMD's CPU product line and mimicked by Intel, signal strong demand and tight supply in the semiconductor market. Geopolitical developments, including easing U.S.-Iran tensions and renewed focus on semiconductor supply chains, also contributed to a broader sector rally.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc Stock (AMD) Moved Down by 5.54% on Mar 26: Facts Behind the Movement
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc (AMD) stock fell by 5.54% on March 26, primarily due to a broader market risk-off sentiment driven by geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, despite strong company fundamentals. The decline followed a previous rally fueled by planned CPU price increases in response to AI and data center demand. While AMD shows strong revenue and AI accelerator deals, it faces intense competitive pressures and sector-wide concerns.

feed.title: ClearOne Offers Free 30-Day Trial of COLLABORATE Space, Its New Cloud Collaboration Service
feed.summary: ClearOne has introduced a free 30-day trial for its new cloud-based collaboration service, COLLABORATE Space. This service aims to unify users with features like virtual meeting rooms, instant messaging, meeting scheduling, recording, and support for up to 25 video and audio participants. Users can also benefit from file sharing, searchable archives, whiteboarding, and the ability to create public and private channels accessible on various devices.

feed.title: Apple's Expanding Enterprise Footprint to Boost Sales: What's Ahead?
feed.summary: Apple is expanding its enterprise footprint, leading to strong adoption of its devices by major companies like Snowflake, AstraZeneca, and Capital One. The company is also launching Apple Business to help enterprises manage devices and connect with local customers. Despite facing competition from Dell Technologies and Alphabet, Apple anticipates significant sales growth in its March quarter, driven by its expanding installed base and new enterprise initiatives.

feed.title: Gartner Forecasts 90% Drop in LLM Inference Costs by 2030
feed.summary: Gartner forecasts that the cost for Large Language Model (LLM) inference will decrease by over 90% by 2030 compared to 2025, primarily due to advancements in semiconductors, infrastructure, and model design. However, this reduction in token costs will not fully translate to enterprise customers, as frontier intelligence and agentic models will demand significantly more tokens, potentially leading to an overall increase in inference costs despite lower unit prices. Gartner advises Chief Product Officers to focus on architectural efficiency and orchestrating workloads across diverse models to avoid being caught in the "commoditization trap."

feed.title: TSEM Stock Faces Heat As GlobalFoundries Files Patent Lawsuits Over Core Chipmaking Technologies
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries has initiated patent lawsuits against Tower Semiconductor in the U.S., alleging unauthorized use of 11 core chipmaking technologies related to manufacturing processes. This legal action, filed with the U.S. International Trade Commission and a federal court, seeks to restrict Tower's product sales and imports in the U.S. and financial compensation. The news caused TSEM stock to drop over 6%, although the company recently announced plans to reorganize its Japan operations by taking full control of its 300mm Fab 7 facility and projected higher-than-estimated Q1 2025 revenue.

feed.title: Cirrus Logic Named Most Innovative in CSR, Barclays Lifts Price Target
feed.summary: Cirrus Logic Inc. has seen a 5.32% stock increase after being named among Fast Company’s ‘Most Innovative Companies’ for its Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) initiatives. Barclays has also raised its price target for Cirrus Logic from $110 to $120, citing updated semiconductor sector estimates and strong handset demand. The company demonstrates robust financial health with $1.9 billion in revenue and a 20.47% profit margin.

feed.title: Chip Shortage 2026: Why CPUs From Intel And AMD Are Getting Harder To Find
feed.summary: A new global chip shortage is impacting CPUs from Intel and AMD, exacerbating an existing supply strain and increasing costs across the tech industry. The shortage is driven by rising demand for AI and general-purpose servers, with geopolitical tensions and material supply risks further tightening the market. This situation is pushing manufacturers towards alternative chip designs like Arm-based processors.

feed.title: Nova, Lattice Semiconductor, IPG Photonics, AMD, and Entegris Shares Plummet, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of several semiconductor companies, including Nova, Lattice Semiconductor, IPG Photonics, AMD, and Entegris, plummeted following news of Google's new TurboQuant algorithm, which is expected to reduce memory requirements for AI models. This pushed concerns about decreased demand for memory chips, overshadowing strong earnings reports from some memory-makers. Additionally, reports of SK Hynix considering a $14 billion U.S. listing further added to competitive pressures in the sector, causing a broad sell-off.

feed.title: A Look At Texas Instruments (TXN) Valuation After IsoShield Power Module Launch And Semiconductor Sector Rally
feed.summary: Texas Instruments (TXN) recently launched new IsoShield power modules, targeting data centers and electric vehicles, amidst a semiconductor sector rally. Despite mixed short-term returns and strong annual revenues, the stock is considered 37.4% undervalued by a key narrative, projecting a fair value of $314.44, while its P/E ratio of 36x is below industry and peer averages but slightly above a fair ratio estimate. The long-term valuation hinges on successful capacity expansion and sustained demand, though market multiples suggest a more cautious outlook.

feed.title: Nova, Lattice Semiconductor, IPG Photonics, AMD, and Entegris Shares Plummet, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of Nova, Lattice Semiconductor, IPG Photonics, AMD, and Entegris fell significantly after Google announced its new TurboQuant algorithm, which threatens to reduce memory requirements for AI models, sparking a sector-wide sell-off in semiconductor stocks. Concerns over increased efficiency leading to decreased demand for memory chips, along with reports of competitor SK Hynix considering a $14 billion U.S. listing, contributed to the market's negative reaction. AMD, in particular, saw a 7.2% drop, despite recent positive news regarding expanded Ryzen AI Embedded processors and the resolution of patent litigation.

feed.title: The Bull Case For SFL (SFL) Could Change Following New US$170 Million Hercules Rig Contract
feed.summary: SFL Corporation has secured a US$170 million drilling contract for its Hercules rig in Canada, which will be operated by Odfjell Drilling. This agreement, spanning a minimum of 400 days, significantly improves the rig's utilization and cash flow visibility, addressing a previous risk concerning the asset's idle time. The new contract, alongside a recent share repurchase program, could stabilize SFL's dividend sustainability and help restore profitability amidst sizable newbuild commitments.

The integration is expected to be crucial for developing competitive AI solutions.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices expects $800M charge due to new U.S. export controls
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) anticipates an $800 million charge related to new U.S. export controls. This charge is primarily due to returns of previously shipped AI chips, including the MI250, as stricter regulations impact trade with China. The company's fourth-quarter revenue guidance of $6.1 billion, with a potential variation of $300 million, will be affected by these developments.

feed.title: GlobalFoundries Files Patent Lawsuits Against Tower Semiconductor - News and Statistics
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries has filed patent infringement lawsuits against Tower Semiconductor in a federal court in Texas and with the U.S. International Trade Commission. GlobalFoundries alleges that Tower Semiconductor infringed upon eleven of its chip manufacturing patents for smartphones and other electronics, and seeks to prevent the importation of certain chips. Both companies are described as specialty chipmakers, differing from larger manufacturers who focus on leading-edge computing chips.

feed.title: AI Tech AMD Launches Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 Dual Edition with Dual-Sided 3D V-Cache
feed.summary: AMD has introduced the Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 Dual Edition, a new desktop processor featuring 3D V-Cache on both chiplets, resulting in a total of 208MB cache. This 16-core CPU, based on Zen 5 architecture, is designed to offer 5-10% performance gains in productivity tasks over its predecessor, the 9950X3D, while maintaining strong gaming performance. Targeting gamers, creators, and developers, the processor supports DDR5-5600 memory and PCIe 5.0 and will be available starting April 22.

feed.title: Will TSM's Aggressive Capex Plan Strengthen Its Foundry Dominance?
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM) plans to invest $52 billion to $56 billion in 2026, a significant increase from 2025, to maintain its lead in advanced chip manufacturing, particularly for AI and high-performance computing. This aggressive capital expenditure, primarily focused on 3-nanometer and 2-nanometer nodes, is coupled with global expansion in the U.S., Germany, Japan, and Taiwan to meet customer demand and secure its supply chain. Despite competition from Intel and GlobalFoundries, TSM aims for 30% revenue growth in 2026, with strong analyst estimates supporting its outlook.

feed.title: 16.40% Autocallable Notes from UBS (AMUB) Linked to AMD Stock
feed.summary: UBS AG is offering Trigger Autocallable Contingent Yield Notes with Memory Interest linked to Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) common stock, featuring a 16.40% per annum contingent coupon and quarterly callable options after six months, maturing on April 12, 2029. Principal repayment at maturity depends on AMD's stock performance relative to a 50% downside threshold. The notes carry significant risks, including potential loss of principal and dependence on UBS's creditworthiness.

feed.title: AMD Drops 2.3% During Broad Market Downturn: Will the Uptrend Continue?

Technical indicators suggest potential support around the 200-day moving average at $194.77, and historical data indicates short-term recovery potential after intraday declines.

feed.title: GlobalFoundries sues Tower Semiconductor, accuses chipmaker of patent infringement
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries (GF) has filed lawsuits against Tower Semiconductor in both the US International Trade Commission and a District Court, accusing the Israeli chipmaker of patent infringement. GF claims Tower has misappropriated its proprietary process technology and infringed 11 US patents related to high-performance technologies. GF is seeking to block the importation and US sale of infringing products and receive compensation for lost profits.

feed.title: Nio CEO William Li Sits Down With Qualcomm China Chair: Cars Are the 'Core Engine' Pushing AI Forward — H

Despite concerns about a looming memory crunch affecting AI infrastructure, Nio is strategically positioning its ET9 flagship as a "mobile living environment" with advanced AI features, coinciding with significant improvements in the company's Q4 financial results.

feed.title: Jasper Therapeutics stock gains 5% on clinical progress despite Q4 loss
feed.summary: Jasper Therapeutics shares rose over 5% in pre-market trading despite reporting a Q4 loss of -$0.32 per share, as investors focused on positive clinical trial updates for its lead candidate, briquilimab. For the full year 2025, Jasper reported a net loss of $75.8 million with cash and cash equivalents of $28.7 million.

feed.title: TD (TD) offers autocallable Notes: AMD- and CRWD-linked $1M tranches
feed.summary: The Toronto-Dominion Bank is offering Trigger Autocallable Contingent Yield Notes linked to the common stock of Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD) and CrowdStrike Holdings, Inc. (CRWD), due September 30, 2027. Each offering is a $1,000,000 tranche, with notes sold at $10 each, promising a high fixed contingent coupon if the underlying stock meets a specified barrier on monthly observation dates. The notes may be automatically called early if the underlying stock reaches a call threshold, otherwise principal repayment at maturity is dependent on the final underlying level relative to a downside threshold, exposing holders to potential principal loss.

feed.title: NVIDIA puts $2B into Marvell to widen AI chip and network ties
feed.summary: NVIDIA announced a strategic partnership with Marvell, investing $2 billion to integrate Marvell into the NVIDIA AI factory and AI-RAN ecosystem through NVLink Fusion. This collaboration aims to provide greater flexibility for customers building next-generation AI infrastructure, with a focus on custom XPUs, NVLink Fusion-compatible networking, and silicon photonics for 5G/6G AI-RAN. The partnership is expected to accelerate rack-scale AI infrastructure and transform telecommunication networks into AI-ready systems.

feed.title: AMD's Gaming Revenues Set to Decelerate: What's the Path Ahead?

AMD faces stiff competition from NVIDIA and Intel in the gaming market, but new product launches and future collaborations like Microsoft's next-gen Xbox are expected to support its gaming business.

feed.title: Bitfarm Plans $161M Bitcoin Sale To Chase AI Boom, Eyes US Relocation
feed.summary: Bitfarms Ltd. (BITF) is rebranding as Keel Infrastructure and will sell $161 million in Bitcoin to shift its focus from crypto mining to high-performance computing (HPC) and artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure. feed.title: Td Synnex Corp's Q1 2026 Earnings Call: Pricing Demand Risks and Hyve Growth Mix Contradict
feed.summary: TD SYNNEX (SNX) reported record Q1 fiscal 2026 non-GAAP gross billings of $25.8 billion, a 24% year-over-year increase, driven by strong growth in both its distribution and Hyve segments. The company provided optimistic Q2 guidance, forecasting non-GAAP gross billings of ~$25.1 billion and non-GAAP diluted EPS of ~$4. However, the earnings call also highlighted several contradictions concerning demand elasticity, Hyve's growth concentration, PC market outlook, Hyve's business line mix, and the assessment of PC demand pull-forward, indicating potential areas of uncertainty in future performance.

feed.title: AMD's Gaming Revenues Set to Decelerate: What's the Path Ahead?

AMD faces tough competition from NVIDIA and Intel, although new product launches and future collaborations, like the next-gen Xbox, offer potential for growth.

feed.title: High‑coupon UBS Notes Linked to AMD (NYSE: AMUB) Maturing 2031

feed.title: Vishay Intertechnology Automotive Grade Photovoltaic MOSFET Driver Boosts Reliability and Lowers Costs in High Voltage Systems
feed.summary: Vishay Intertechnology has released a new Automotive Grade photovoltaic MOSFET driver, the VODA1275, designed to enhance safety and reliability in high voltage automotive systems. This device, featuring an 8 mm creepage distance and CTI 600 mold compound, offers industry-leading turn-on times, open circuit voltage, and short circuit current. It simplifies designs and reduces costs by allowing a single driver to replace two in applications requiring higher voltages, making it ideal for EV and HEV pre-charge circuits, wall chargers, and battery management systems.

feed.title: SEALSQ and Kaynes Semicon Mark a Major Milestone in the Development of India's First Post-Quantum Cryptography Personalization Center with the Inauguration of Kaynes' New OSAT Facility in Sanand, Gujarat
feed.summary: SEALSQ and Kaynes Semicon have announced a significant milestone with the inauguration of Kaynes Semicon's OSAT facility in Sanand, Gujarat, which will house India's first Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) Personalization Center. This center will enable the sovereign, large-scale personalization of quantum-safe semiconductor devices on Indian soil, eliminating the need to ship devices abroad for security provisioning. The initiative, formalized through the joint venture SEALKAYNESQ Ltd, aims to secure India's digital infrastructure against future quantum threats using SEALSQ's QS7001 platform, which implements NIST-standardized post-quantum algorithms.

feed.title: AMD Delivers Breakthrough MLPerf Inference 6.0 Results

These achievements position AMD as a strong contender in the generative AI inference market, with a clear roadmap for future Instinct GPU series and rack-scale solutions.

The surge was primarily driven by strong technical indicators and robust ETF activity, propelling AMD above key moving averages. Analysts suggest a continued bullish outlook, with opportunities in leveraged ETFs and call options if the stock maintains its upward trajectory.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. stock: Why it's drawing investor attention now

The company's focus on innovative CPUs and GPUs for data centers and premium client segments, along with analyst upgrades, is attracting significant investor attention. While competitive pressures and valuation concerns exist, AMD's growth trajectory in the AI revolution makes it a key stock to watch for global investors.

feed.title: Broadcom shares jump on long-term AI chip and networking deals with Google
feed.summary: Broadcom shares rose after agreements with Google were revealed. The agreements include co-development of custom Tensor Processing Units (TPUs) for Google's future AI models and a supply assurance for networking and other components for Google's next-generation AI racks through 2031. This collaboration also extends to third-party AI developers like Anthropic PBC, which will access TPU-based compute capacity through Broadcom, solidifying Broadcom's role in the AI supply chain.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Maintains Overweight Rating for Advanced Micro Devices (AMD)
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has reaffirmed its Overweight rating and $345 price target for Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), highlighting a favorable setup ahead of the company's Q1 results, driven by strong demand for EPYC server CPUs and new AI GPU announcements. UBS also reiterated a Buy rating and $310 price target, citing confidence in AMD's revenue growth for 2027 and potential for additional gigawatt-scale AI deals beyond OpenAI and Meta Platforms. The semiconductor company is seen as a compelling investment due to upcoming MI450 shipments and its diverse product segments including data center, client & gaming, and embedded.

feed.title: Analog Devices Expands Board with Dr. Yoky Matsuoka Appointment
feed.summary: Analog Devices (ADI) has appointed Dr. Yoky Matsuoka to its board as an independent director, expanding the board to 11 members, effective January 20, 2026. Dr. Matsuoka's background at leading tech companies like Google and Apple, coupled with her expertise in hardware, software, and AI integration, is expected to significantly bolster ADI's innovation and market competitive edge. This strategic move comes as ADI achieved a 52-week high, reflecting strong investor confidence.

feed.title: Intel Corp Stock (INTC) Closed Up by 8.84% on Apr 1: A Full Analysis
feed.summary: Intel Corp (INTC) saw an 8.84% stock increase on April 1, 2026, primarily due to its strategic repurchase of Apollo Global Management's 49% equity in its Fab 34 facility for $14.2 billion, signaling renewed confidence in its manufacturing ambitions. This positive movement was further fueled by advancements in AI technology and a growing semiconductor industry. Despite some analyst caution and geopolitical risks, investor sentiment is shifting towards "execution" for Intel, with Q1 2026 financial results anticipated on April 23.

feed.title: Analyst Upgrades Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) to ‘Hold’
feed.summary: Erste Group has upgraded Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) to a 'Buy' rating from 'Hold' due to the semiconductor company's strong performance, attractive product portfolio, and anticipated demand for its new MI450 series. AMD reported record annual revenue of $34.6 billion for FY 2025 and aims for $9.8 billion in Q1 2026, driven by high-performance CPU and GPU demand. The analyst highlights the company's solid adjusted operating margin and competitive product launches.

feed.title: Micron Technology (MU) Gets Downgraded by Analyst
feed.summary: Micron Technology (MU) has been downgraded from 'Buy' to 'Hold' by Erste Group due to concerns about high investment requirements for production expansion, which will negatively impact free cash flow, and worries regarding the long-term sustainability of the memory cycle. Micron also plans significant capital expenditure increases for fiscal years 2026 and 2027. The company's share price has fallen over 20% since March 17, as the market reevaluates memory pricing and AI-driven demand.

feed.title: Shoe Carnival Set to Release Q4 Earnings on March 26
feed.summary: Shoe Carnival (SCVL) is scheduled to release its Q4 earnings on March 26, with an anticipated EPS of $0.33, marking a significant year-over-year decline. Revenue is also expected to decrease by 3.5% to $253.75 million, signaling sales performance challenges, despite the company's historical trend of often beating EPS estimates. Analyst revisions indicate a cautious outlook, with two upward and one downward EPS revision, and two downward revenue revisions in the past three months.

feed.title: Get 32GB of Corsair Vengeance RAM for less than $200 when bundled with AMD's 9800X3D and X870-E motherboard — free liquid cooler and a copy of Crimson Desert to kickstart a top AM5 gaming build
feed.summary: Newegg is offering a significant bundle deal for PC builders looking for an AM5 gaming rig, combining AMD's Ryzen 7 9800X3D processor, an MSI MPG X870E Carbon Wi-Fi motherboard, and 32GB of Corsair Vengeance DDR5-6400 RAM for $1,091, a savings of over $200 on individual component pricing. The bundle also includes a free 240mm MSI Mag Coreliquid cooler and a copy of the game Crimson Desert, adding further value. This deal provides a powerful foundation for a high-end gaming PC, featuring the regarded Ryzen 7 9800X3D and high-performance RAM, making it an attractive option given current RAM prices.

feed.title: The Sovereign of Silicon: A 2026 Deep-Dive into TSMC (NYSE: TSM)
feed.summary: This article provides a deep dive into Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) in 2026, highlighting its technological dominance with 2nm mass production and a market capitalization of $1.75 trillion. It explores TSMC's business model, strong financial performance, and key innovations like GAA transistors, while also examining the geopolitical risks, energy challenges, and significant costs associated with its global expansion. The piece concludes that despite being a high-growth AI play, an investment in TSMC is also a bet on global stability.

feed.title: End-to-End AI Defense: Check Point Unveils Security Blueprint for Infrastructure and LLMs
feed.summary: Check Point Software Technologies has released its AI Factory Security Architecture Blueprint, providing a comprehensive, vendor-tested reference architecture to secure private AI infrastructure from hardware to application layers. This blueprint, built on Check Point's firewall and AI security technologies and NVIDIA BlueField DPUs, addresses the unique security challenges of AI data centers, which have become highly valuable and vulnerable assets. It includes a multi-layered defense strategy covering perimeter, application/LLM, AI infrastructure, and workload/container layers to protect against threats like data poisoning, model theft, and prompt injection.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Stock: Diverging Analyst Perspectives Before Q1 Earnings
feed.summary: Ahead of its Q1 earnings, Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is receiving mixed analyst perspectives. While Wells Fargo and UBS maintain optimistic outlooks with high price targets, citing strong demand for EPYC processors and potential AI infrastructure partnerships, Citi has reduced its price objective. This divergence highlights a split in confidence regarding AMD's near-term performance, despite its growing server CPU market share.

feed.title: Qualcomm Snapdragon X2 Elite Extreme Review: New ASUS And HP Laptops Tested

feed.title: Supermicro owes its rapid rise to $4 trillion Nvidia—but China smuggling allegations and a high-profile arrest could blow up the partnership
feed.summary: Supermicro's crucial partnership with Nvidia is jeopardized by recent China smuggling allegations and the arrest of co-founder Yih-Shyan “Wally” Liaw. Liaw is accused of smuggling $2.5 billion worth of Nvidia-powered servers to China, actions Supermicro claims it was a victim of. The scandal raises "credibility issues" for Supermicro, with analysts questioning the company's governance and calling for new leadership, while Nvidia faces decisions about maintaining its significant relationship with Supermicro.

feed.title: Samsung joins world's top earners: Why its market value still trails Nvidia, TSMC
feed.summary: Samsung Electronics Co. reported a record 755% surge in quarterly earnings, placing it fourth globally behind Apple, Nvidia, and Microsoft. Despite this strong performance, driven by demand for memory chips for AI, Samsung's market value still lags behind giants like Nvidia and TSMC. The company has secured significant deals, including supplying HBM4 chips to AMD and manufacturing Nvidia's new Groq 3 LPU, highlighting its crucial role in the AI semiconductor market.

feed.title: Nvidia’s (NVDA) Deal With Competitor Marvell (MRVL) Only Strengthens Its Own Ecosystem, Here’s How
feed.summary: NVIDIA Corporation has announced a strategic partnership with Marvell Technology, including a $2 billion investment, to integrate Marvell's custom AI accelerators (XPUs) with NVIDIA's NVLink Fusion AI infrastructure. This collaboration aims to strengthen NVIDIA's AI ecosystem by combining Marvell's XPUs and interconnect solutions with NVIDIA's extensive suite of supporting technologies. Analysts view this deal as a significant move for both companies, boosting Marvell with the investment and further solidifying NVIDIA's position in the AI market.

feed.title: Nutanix and Netapp Form Strategic Alliance with New Integration for A Modern Cloud Platform
feed.summary: Nutanix and NetApp have announced a strategic alliance to integrate NetApp's Intelligent Data Infrastructure with the Nutanix Cloud Platform (NCP) solution, utilizing the Nutanix AHV hypervisor. This collaboration aims to provide customers with enhanced flexibility and choice for optimizing their virtualization and data strategies across on-premises, cloud, and containerized environments. The integration promises benefits such as modernized virtualization, faster VM migration, simplified operations, VM-granular management, and built-in cyber resilience.

feed.title: Digital Realty Opens First Asia Pacific Innovation Lab in Japan to Help Accelerate AI and Hybrid Cloud Deployment
feed.summary: Digital Realty has opened its first Asia Pacific Innovation Lab (DRIL) in Tokyo, Japan, aimed at accelerating AI and hybrid cloud deployments. This facility, established through a joint venture with Mitsubishi Corporation, provides a production-grade environment for partners and customers to test and optimize high-density AI and hybrid cloud architectures. It features advanced cooling capabilities and is part of a global network of DRILs designed to support AI-ready infrastructure and address the growing demand for complex AI workloads.

feed.title: Citi Bullish on AMD and Analog Devices, Sees Resilience in Apple Supply Chain
feed.summary: Citi analysts have initiated an "upside catalyst watch" on AMD and Analog Devices, expressing strong confidence in their near-term stock performance. AMD is expected to benefit from increased demand for server CPUs driven by "agentic AI" and growing market share, while Analog Devices is poised for recovery through cost-led price increases in the analog chip sector. The report also highlights resilience in Apple's supply chain, with suppliers like Skyworks Solutions and Qualcomm seen as more defensive due to their exposure to high-end market demand.

feed.title: Onvo L90 to adopt Nio's in-house developed Shenji smart driving chip, report says
feed.summary: Nio's sub-brand Onvo will reportedly use the company's in-house developed Shenji smart driving chip in the updated 2026 L90 SUV, signaling Nio's accelerated integration of its semiconductor technology into its sub-brands and a transition away from Nvidia chips. The move aims to enhance the L90's smart driving system, aligning it with Nio's flagship models and potentially introducing a LiDAR-equipped version. This shift comes as Nio seeks financial returns from its costly R&D in chip development, with its chip subsidiary Shenji recently completing a significant funding round.

feed.title: Analog Devices and NXP Semiconductors Shares Skyrocket, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Analog Devices (NASDAQ: ADI) and NXP Semiconductors (NASDAQ: NXPI) saw their shares jump significantly after the VanEck Semiconductor ETF rose due to the de-escalation of the U.S.-Iran conflict. The reopening of the Strait of Hormuz is crucial for the semiconductor industry, as it facilitates the transit of noble gases and materials essential for chip fabrication. Both companies experienced notable gains, with Analog Devices up 26.4% year-to-date, indicating investor confidence in the sector amid positive geopolitical news and strong government support for the chip industry.
